===BEGIN LEAN PROOF===
import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic
open Nat Int

-- Define functions
def random := fn () : Int =>
  let x := nondet_int
  if x < 0 then -x else x

def loop (a b : Int) : Unit :=
  if hA > hB then do
    let newB := b + a
    let newA := a + 1
    loop newA newB
  else Unit.default

-- Main function
def main : Unit := do
  let a := -random()
  let b := -random()
  loop a b

-- Property to check
theorem not_overflow : ∀ a b, a > b → a + 1 > b
| a b hA => by rw [hA]
  let hNextA := a + 1
  have hNextA_gt_b := assume h : hNextA ≤ b
  have h : a + 1 > b := by contradiction
  { rw [h] at hNextA,
    exact lt_trans hNextA hNextA_gt_b }

-- Initialize main function and check property
initMain := main
check (initMain, ∀ a b : Int, a > b → a + 1 > b)
===END LEAN PROOF===

open Mathlib
open Nat
open Option

inductive Et1 : Type :=
| main : Nat → Nat → Et1

def random : Rand (Option Nat) := do ℕ

def loop (a b : Nat) : Et1 := if h : a > b then do {
    let a' := a + 1
    let b' := b + a
    Et1.main a' b'
  } else Et1.main a b

def main : Et1 := do {
    let x ← random
    let y ← random
    Et1.main (-x) (-y)
}

#print "Lemmas"

lemma init_main : Init main := by apply_rules [init_def, init_main_def]

lemma ltl_not_overflow : LTL (fun (a b : Nat) : Prop := a > b) (fun i : Nat => i ≠ 0) (fun i : Nat => Option.map (λ x => Option.map (λ y => Some (a, b)) (random i)) (main)) :=
begin
  rintros i,
  induction i with i iih,
  { simp only [Option.map_none, Option.map_some, ltl_false, ltl_true, Option.bind_none, Option.bind_some] },
  { simp only [Option.map_bind, Option.map_bind, ltl_and, Nat.succ_eq_add, Nat.add_comm, Nat.add_comm, ltl_and, Nat.add_assoc, Nat.add_comm, Nat.add_comm, ltl_and, Nat.add_assoc, Nat.add_comm, Nat.add_comm, Nat.add_comm] },
end

#check (init_main () Ltl.&& ltl_not_overflow)
===END LEAN PROOF===

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic
open Nat

-- Definition of id function
def id (x : Nat) : Nat := if h : x = 0 then 0 else id (x - 1) + 1

-- Property definition
lemma id_property : ∀ (n : Nat), n ≠ 0 → id n = n + (id (n - 1))
intros n h
induction n generalizing h
case 0
  simp only [id, h, zero_add, succ_eq_zero]
case (succ n)
  have h_rec : id (n - 1) = n + id (n - 2)
  by induction n generalizing h
  simp only [h, h_rec, succ_add_succ, succ_eq_add_one, succ_eq_add]

-- Main function definition
def main : IO Unit := do
  let input ← readNat "Input:"
  let result ← pure (id input)
  unless (result = input + (id (input - 1))) do
    error "ID function does not return expected value"
-- Checking the property
check (main) $ ∀ _, id_property.mpr _
===END LEAN PROOF===

-- Define main function
def main : IO Unit := do
  let input := 10
  let result := id input
  if result == 10 then do
    io.putStrLn "Error: result is not 10"
    exit! 1
  pure unit

-- Define error function for simplicity
def reach_error : IO Unit := do
  fail "reach_error"

-- Define the property to check
def prop : Bool := ∀ _, ∀ _, ¬ result.val

-- Translate the C code to Lean
def c_code : IO Unit := do
  let input := ℕ.toInt <| 10
  let result := id input
  unless (result = 10) do
    reach_error

-- Translate the property to Lean
def ltl_formula : Prop := ∀ s : Stream Nat, ∀ i, i < s.length → ¬ result.val

-- Check the property holds for the given initial state and the main function
def init_state : IO Unit := do
  let g : Stream Nat := repeatN 10 Nat.succ
  pure g

-- Main proof
check (init_state >>= fun g => main >>= fun () => ltl_formula g))
===END LEAN PROOF===

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classical
import Mathlib.Tactic.Core
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Function
import Mathlib.Tactic.Nonclassical

open Function
open Lean
open Nat
open Expression
open Ltl

namespace Id

def id (x : Int) : Int :=
  if hx : x = 0 then
    0
  else
    id x.pred + 1

def main : IO Unit := do
  let input := 10
  let result := id input
  unless (result = 11) do
    abort "id function did not return the correct value"

def initMain : StateM IO Unit := do
  pure unit

def callReachError : IO Unit := do
  io.stderr.putStrLn "ERROR: Reached error state"
  abort

def G_not_call_reach_error : Prop := ∀ i, ¬ (i = 4 ∧ callReachError)

def Ltl_G_not_call_reach_error : Prop := ∀ σ : Signals.Signal IO Unit, G ∨ (∃ i, ¬ G i ∧ σ i), ∀ i, ¬ callReachError i

def proof : Prop := initMain >>= main >> Ltl_G_not_call_reach_error

-- Prove the property using classical reasoning
theorem init_main_main : Init main >>= main = proof.elim
  (fun i h => Classical.choice Elim.inr (Classical.choice Elim.inr h.2))
  (fun h => Classical.choice Elim.inl h)

theorem Ltl_init_main_main : LTL.G (fun σ => ∀ i, ¬ callReachError i) (initMain >>= main >>= fun _ => σ) = proof
  rw [← Init.init, ← Monad.bind, ← Monad.bind]
  apply Ltl.and'
  { intros i h
    apply Classical.not
    exact h.2.elim Rfl }
  { intros i h
    apply Ltl.and'
    apply Ltl.next
    exact Classical.choice Elim.inl (Classical.choice Elim.inl h)
    exact Ltl.next _
    exact Classical.choice Elim.inr (Classical.choice Elim.inr Rfl) }

-- Check the property
check (init_main_main)
-- Check the LTL property
check (Ltl_init_main_main)
===END LEAN PROOF===

===BEGIN LEAN PROOF===
import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic
open Nat

-- Define functions and predicates from C code
def reach_error := λ_, abort!
def ERROR := fun _ => reach_error _

-- Main function
def f (n : Nat) : Unit :=
  if hNat.le n 2 then return ()
  -- Decrement n and recursively call f
  let dec_n := n - 1
  f dec_n
  -- Error handling
  ERROR

-- Main function in Lean, returning Unit
def init_main : Unit := f 2

-- Property to check: no overflow during recursive call of f
lemma no_overflow_during_f_call : ∀ n : Nat, n < 3 → ∀ dec_n : Nat, dec_n ≤ n,
  init_main () ∧ LTL.always (fun s => s.2 ≠ Some overflow) (init_main.get_state)
:= begin
  classical
  intros n hn dec_n hdec_le
  have h_f_call_correct := by simp [hn, hdec_le]
  have h_state_after_f_call := by simp [h_f_call_corrent]
  have h_state_after_init := by simp [init_main]
  have h_no_overflow := by simpa only [h_state_after_init, h_state_after_f_call] using h_no_overflow_after_dec
  exact ⟨init_main (), LTL.mk always h_no_overflow⟩
end

-- Lemma: no overflow after decrementing a number
lemma no_overflow_after_dec (n : Nat) : n < 3 → ∀ dec_n : Nat, dec_n ≤ n → ∥dec_n∥ = n → ∥dec_n∥ ≠ overflow
:= begin
  intros n hn dec_n hdec_le h_eq
  rcases h_eq with rfl | h
  { simp [h] }
  { simp [h]
    rw [sub_eq_zero] at hdec_le ⊢
    simp [hdec_le] }
end
===END LEAN PROOF===

begin
  assume hx : x ≥ 0, hy : y ≥ 0, hx' : 0 < x, hyt : 0 < y, h : main = 0,

  have h'' : x ≤ 65535, from and.right hx,

===BEGIN LEAN PROOF===
#printing "C code not directly translatable to Lean, need to define the semantics of C code in Lean first." in info

open Lean Nat Int Enum Bool
open List
open Function

universe u v w

-- Define C integer and boolean types
inductive CInt : Type u := | CInt (i : Int)
inductive CBool : Type u := | CBool (b : Bool)

-- Define C code state
record CState : Type u := { x : CInt, y : CInt }

-- Define C code transition relation
def CTrans (s : CState) (d : CState) : Prop :=
  (s.x.get_nat = s.y.get_nat) ∧
  (s.y.get_nat < 0) ∧
  (d.x.get_nat = s.x.get_nat + s.y.get_nat) ∧
  (d.y.get_nat = -2 * s.y.get_nat - 1) ∧
  (∀ i, ∥s.x.get_nat <= i.get_nat∥ ∧ ∥i.get_nat < s.x.get_nat -> i.get_nat < d.x.get_nat∥)

-- Define initial state
def initState : CState := { x := CInt 0, y := CInt 0 }

-- Define the C code as a transition system
def CCode : Type u := TransitionSystem CState CTrans initState

-- Define the property to be checked in LTL
def LTLProperty : Prop := ∀ s : CState, ∃ t : CState, Transition s t → ¬ F s

-- Check the property
check (initState, LTLProperty)
===END LEAN PROOF===

===BEGIN LEAN PROOF===
#printing Off

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Core
import Mathlib.Tactic.SpaèsDansLeNoir

open Lean
open Nat
open Function
open Option

/- This function generates a sequence of pairs (x, y) based on the given C code. -/
def cCodeSequence : IO (Array (Int × Int)) := do
  let x := randomInt (-65535) 65535
  let y := randomInt (-65535) 65535
  let mut currState := Array.ofList [(x, y)]
  while x ≥ 0 do
    x := x + y
    y := -2 * y - 1
    currState := Array.push currState (x, y)
  pure currState

/- This lemma states that the sequence generated by the C code is infinite and monotonically increasing. -/
lemma cCodeSequenceIsMonotonicallyIncreasing (seq : Array (Int × Int)) :
  ∀ i i' < length seq, i < i' → seq.get i.1 < seq.get i'.1 := by
  intros i i' h
  have x₁ := get_fst <| seq.get i
  have y₁ := get_snd <| seq.get i
  have x₂ := get_fst <| seq.get i'
  have y₂ := get_snd <| seq.get i'
  have hx := lt_of_le (le_refl x₁) (lt_of_le h (le_refl x₂))
  have hy := lt_of_le y₁ y₂
  exact and_of_bools (hx) hy

/- This lemma states that the negation of the given property holds at the initial state. -/
lemma cCodeInitIsFalse (): ¬CHECK (init (main()), LTL (F end)) := by
  have seq := ⟨cCodeSequence⟩
  have is_monotonic := cCodeSequenceIsMonotonicallyIncreasing seq
  have neg_ltl_end := ¬LTL (F end)
  have neg_init := ¬CHECK (init (main()), neg_ltl_end)
  exact and_of_bools is_monotonic neg_init

/- The main proof. We show that the given property holds by negating it and proving that the negation is false. -/
theorem main : CHECK (init (main()), LTL (F end)) := by
  have neg_prop := ¬CHECK (init (main()), LTL (F end))
  exact neg_prop

#align init main
def main := by
  print "Starting simulation..."
  let _ := cCodeSequence
  have prop := CHECK (init (main()), LTL (F end))
  have _ := print (if prop then "Property holds." else "Property does not hold.")
  pure unit

#align main _main
def _main := main

#align init _main
def _init := do
  set_option linter.uppercase false
  set_option linter.multiline false
  set_option pp.all true
  set_option pp.depth 50
  set_option pp.show_proof_nroff false
  initRunMcmm1
  #align in main _main
  _main
===END LEAN PROOF===

===BEGIN LEAN PROOF===
import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic
open Nat

-- Define functions and lemmas from the C code
def id (x : Int) : Nat :=
  if h : x = 0 then 0
  else id' (x - 1) + 1
and id' (n : Nat) : Nat := if n > 5 then 5 else id' (n - 1) + 1

lemma id_le_five (x : Int) : id x ≤ 5 := begin
  induction x with hx; simp [id] [hx]; apply le_five
end

-- Define a predicate for the C state
structure CState :=
  (input : Int)
  (result : Int)

-- Define the main function and the property to be checked
def main (cs : CState) : Prop :=
  cs.result ≠ 10

lemma init_main (): init (fun cs => (main cs)) = true := rfl

-- Define the LTL property to be checked
def G_not_call_reach_error (cs : CState) : Prop :=
  ∀ (i : Nat), i < cs.result → cs.result ≠ 10

-- The main proof
lemma main_property : LTL (G not_call_reach_error) (init (fun cs => (main cs))) := begin
  have h : ∀ (cs : CState), main cs → cs.result ≠ 10 := begin
    induction cs with hcs; intros; assumption
  end,
  have h' : ∀ (i : Nat), i < cs.input → cs.input ≠ 10 := begin
    intros i hi; assumption
  end,
  have h'' : ∀ (i : Nat), i < (id cs.input) → id cs.input ≠ 10 := begin
    induction hcs with hcs hcs_is_main; intros i hi; simp only [id_le_five, h'] at hcs_is_main;
    rw [hcs_is_main] at hi; simp [hi]
  end,
  have h''' : ∀ i, i < (id cs.input) → cs.result ≠ 10 := begin
    intros i hi; simp only [h] at h; apply h _ _ i hi
  end,
  have h'''' : ∀ i, i < cs.input → cs.result ≠ 10 := begin
    intros i hi; simp only [h''] at h'''; apply h''' _ _ i h'''; simp [h''']
  end,
  apply LTL_G_imp h''' h''''
end
===END LEAN PROOF===

open Lean.Elab.Tactics
open Lean.Expr.Tactics
open Lean.Syntax
open Lean.Parser.Tactics

-- Define id function
def id (x : Int) : Option Int :=
if x = 0 then some 0
else do let rec h x' : Option Int := match x' with
  | (unary-- `nat.succ _) x => h (x-- 1)
  | _ => none
in some (unary-- `nat.succ (h x-- 1).get.get)

-- Define main function
def main (): Option Unit := do
  let input := (unary-- `nat.castNat (expression.constant (nat.toInt <| expression.mkConst <| "VERIFIER_nondet_int"--> expression.anonymous))).get
  let result := id input
  match result with
  | some res => if res = 10 then fail! "Reached error state" else pure ()
  | none => pure ()

-- Define helper function to check property
def checkProperty : Expression → Expression → Expression := λ (p : Expression) (q : Expression),
  let t1 := lean_expr_to_term p
  let t2 := lean_expr_to_term q
  let l1 := lamma "init_main" [] [("main"--> expr.constName), ("init"--> expr.constName)] [] (expr.app (expr.const "CHECK") [t1, t2])
  l1

-- Define goal expression
let goalExpr := checkProperty (init--> expression.constName) (LTL.G--> expression.constName (expression.unaryOp-- `bnot--> expression.const "call"--> expression.constName (expression.const "reach_error"--> expression.constName)))

-- Define initial state
let initialState := expression.anonymous

-- Define init and main expressions
let init := letcode fun () => do
  main--> expression
  pure ()
in letcode fun () => init--> expression

-- Solve the problem using tactic
run_tactic fun () => do
  set_option tactic.norm_num l_bhk.normalizationMode.all
  let state ← initialState
  let goal ← goalExpr
  have h1 := goal.get_lhs--> toExpression
  have h2 := goal.get_rhs--> toExpression
  have h3 ← expr.in_set state (initialState--> toExpression)--> tactic.interactive
  have h4 ← expr.in_set state (init--> toExpression)--> tactic.interactive
  have h5 ← Lean.Elab.Tactics.mk_expr ``LTL.G h3--> toExpression--> tactic.interactive
  have h6 ← Lean.Elab.Tactics.mk_expr ``LTL.call h5 ``h4--> toExpression--> tactic.interactive
  have h7 ← Lean.Elab.Tactics.mk_expr ``LTL.G h5 h6--> toExpression--> tactic.interactive
  have h8 ← Lean.Elab.Tactics.mk_expr ``LTL.not ``h2--> toExpression--> tactic.interactive
  have h9 ← Lean.Elab.Tactics.mk_expr ``LTL.not ``h8--> toExpression--> tactic.interactive
  have h10 ← Lean.Elab.Tactics.mk_expr ``LTL.G h1 h9--> toExpression--> tactic.interactive
  have h11 ← Lean.Elab.Tactics.assert h10--> tactic.interactive
  pure ()

  have h_lt_n := lt_of_int_le (int.to_nat j) n'
  have h_lt_1025 := lt_of_int_le (int.to_nat j) 1024
  let next := do ␣
    have h_le_n := le_total (le_refl i) (le_succ i)
    let a_val := a.get! i
    have h_eq_zero := by simp [h_le_n, h_lt_n]
    match h_eq_zero with

        pure unit
    | none => do ␣

  let n := if k then init else succ 1023

/- LTL Property -/
lemma ltl_property : ∀ (s : Stream Nat), LTL (G fun _ => s.last ≠ 42) s,
  from ∀ (n : Nat), s.head = n → s.last ≠ 42 → s.tail = s, by
  intros s h_h s h_ltl h_tl
  apply LTL.mono
  { intros n hn, apply h_h }
  { intros hs t ht, apply h_tl hs t ht }

/- Check init function and main function -/

  from rfl

/- Property to be proven -/
theorem init_main : ∀ k : Bool, ∀ n : Nat, ∀ i : Nat, ∀ j : Nat, ∀ a : Array Nat 1024,
  LTL.always (fun t => ¬ (reach_error t)) (main k n i j a) → init (main k n i j a)

-- Define functions from C code
def plus_z (x : Int) (z : Int) : Int := x + z
def times_two_y (y : Int) : Int := 2 * y

-- Define the main function
def main : Nat := do
  let x ← int.to_nat <$> (arithmetic.randomInt ℤ (-2 ^ 31) 2 ^ 31)
  let y ← int.to_nat <$> (arithmetic.randomInt ℤ (-2 ^ 31) 2 ^ 31)
  let z ← int.to_nat <$> (arithmetic.randomInt ℤ (-2 ^ 31) 2 ^ 31)
  let mut acc : Int := x
  while acc < 0 do
    acc := plus_z acc z
    z := times_two_y z
    let _ := pure y
    y := y + 1
  pure 0

-- Property to check: !overflow
lemma main_no_overflow : ∀ x y z, main () = 0 → x < 0 → y ≠ 0 → z ≠ -1 → ¬(x + z < sint.from_nat (2 ^ 31 - 1)) :=
begin
  intros x y z hmain hx hy hz
  -- Base case: x >= 0
  have base : x ≥ 0 → x + z ≥ 0 := by assumption
  rcases hx with (hx | hx')
  {simpa using base} -- hx is true, no need to prove anything
  -- Inductive step
  induction x with hx
  -- Base case: x = 0
  simp [hz, hx] -- x = 0, z ≠ -1, so x + z ≥ 0
  -- Inductive step: x = succ p
  have hx_plus_one : x + 1 > 0 := nat.succ_pos p
  have hx_plus_one_z : x + 1 + z > 0 := add_pos hx_plus_one hz
  simpa [hx_plus_one_z] using hx_plus_one
end

-- Main entry point
initMain <| main _ _ _
apply main_no_overflow hmain hx hy hz with 𝕙(fun h => sorry "Please provide a counterexample for this hypothesis: " ++ string_of_string h)
---END LEAN PROOF---

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic

-- Define the array type and assumptions about its initial values
inductive array (n : ℕ) (T : Type) : Type :=
| nil : array T
| cons : T → array T → array T

variable (a : array 1048 int)

-- Assumption: all initial elements of the array are non-negative
hypothesis init_nonneg : ∀ i : ℕ, 0 ≤ a i

-- Define the predicate "a[1+2] >= 0"
def a_ge_zero : Prop := ∀ i, a (1 + 2) ≤ i → 0 ≤ a 3

-- Main theorem: property G is satisfied
theorem main_property : G a_ge_zero
-- Proof by induction on the number of iterations
induction h: nat default 0
-- Base case: when no iterations have been performed
case (0)
  have : a_ge_zero.1 a 0 := init_nonneg a 0 -- a[1+2] is non-negative at the beginning
  exact a_ge_zero.2 (0 : ℕ) a_ge_zero.1

-- Inductive step: assume the property holds for n iterations, prove it holds for n+1
case (succ h)
  have : a (1 + 2) ≤ h := by induction h with i hi; assumption
  have : a_ge_zero.1 a h := h.1 i
  have a_3_updated : a 3 = a 2 + 1 - 1 := by assumption
  have a_3_new : 0 ≤ a 3 := by simp [a_3_updated, init_nonneg a (2 + 1)]
  have a_1_2_new : a 1 + 2 = h := by simp [h]
  have a_ge_zero_new : a_ge_zero (cons a a_ge_zero) :=
    and.intro a_3_new (a_ge_zero h a_ge_zero) -- induction hypothesis
  have a_ge_zero_new_2 : a_ge_zero (cons a_3 a_ge_zero_new) :=
    and.intro a_ge_zero_new (a_ge_zero a_ge_zero_new) -- apply the function to a[3]
  have a_ge_zero_new_3 : a_ge_zero (cons a a_ge_zero_new_2) :=
    and.intro a_ge_zero_new_2 (a_ge_zero a_ge_zero_new) -- apply the function to a[1+2]
  exact a_ge_zero_new_3

-- Global checking: property G is globally satisfied
check a_ge_zero

import Mathlib

open Lean Nat

-- Define the array type and the ranking function
def array (n : Nat) := Array (Nat) n

def ranking_func (a : array 1048) : Nat := a.get! 3

-- Assume the array is initialized by the main function
def init_main := fun a => do
  let mut i := 0
  while i < 1048 && a.get! i >= 0 do
    let mut temp := a.get! i
    a.set! i := a.get! (i + 1) - 1
    a.set! (i + 1) := temp
    i := i + 1
  return a

-- Check the property using the given main function
theorem init_main_property : init (init_main) = true
begin
  apply init.intros
  apply init_main
  simp
end

-- Define the LTL property
definition G_def_behavior : Prop := ∀ n, ∀ i, ∀ a, (i < n) → ranking_func a = a.get! (i + 1) - 1

-- Check the property for the given main function
theorem main_property : CHECK (init_main, G_def_behavior)
begin
  apply CHECK.intros
  apply init_main_property
  intro n i a h_i
  have h_ranking := ranking_func a
  simp only [h_i] at h_ranking
  have h_next_elem := a.get! (i + 1)
  have h_next_ranking := ranking_func (update! i h_next_elem a)
  simp only [h_ranking, update_get] at h_next_ranking
  simp only [h_next_elem, h_next_ranking]
  rw [sub_succ, sub_sub]
  exact h_i
end

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic
open Function
open Array
open Lean
open Expressions
open Std.Array

universe u v

variable (α : Type u) [DecidableEq α]
variable (N : ℕ)

def main (a : Array α N) : ℕ :=
do {
  let mut arr : Array α N := a
  for i in ∀-range N do
    for j in ∀-range N do
      for k in ∀-range N do
        arr := arr.map (λ x, x.1 + arr.get! j)
  let a := arr.get 0
  if a ≠ 1 then do
    reach_error
    abort
  return arr.get 100
}

def init (main : Array α N) : Prop :=
∧3 a i j k (a = 1) ∧ a = main (main.get i).1

def valid_free : Prop :=
∀ i j, i ≠ j → (main.get i).1 = (main.get j).1 → main.get i = main.get j

def valid_deref : Prop :=
∀ i, main.get i = (main.map get).get i

def valid_memtrack : Prop :=
∀ i, i < N → main.get i = (main.take (i + 1)).slice! 0 i

/- Prove the given properties -/
@[simp] theorem init_main : init main = true := by simp

theorem valid_free_main : valid_free main :=
begin
  intros i j hne
  have h_i : (main.get i).1 = (main.map get).get i := valid_deref _ _
  have h_j : (main.get j).1 = (main.map get).get j := valid_deref _ _
  rw [h_i, h_j, hne]
end

theorem valid_deref_main : valid_deref main :=
begin
  intros i
  rw [valid_deref _ _]
end

theorem valid_memtrack_main : valid_memtrack main :=
begin
  intros i hi
  have h_take := valid_memtrack _ _
  let n := main.length
  have h_get_i := main.get_unsafe i
  have h_slice_i := h_take.slice_eq! i (i + 1)
  rw [h_slice_i, h_get_i] at hi
end

/- Check the properties -/
CHECK (init_main)
CHECK (valid_free_main)
CHECK (valid_deref_main)
CHECK (valid_memtrack_main)
===END LEAN PROOF===

  let arr : Array ℕ ℕ := init.1
  set_option tt.interactive.mode `omit_initial_unsolved_goals
  let a' := ref 1
  let mut arr' := arr
  for i in ℕ do
    for j in ℕ do
      for k in ℕ do
        arr'.update i (arr'.update j (add a arr'.get j))
  alter_state fun s => ∥ a' := a'.get 1
  have valid_free : ∀ i, arr'.get i = arr.get (i.toNat) := begin
    by { induction i with i IH }
  end
  have valid_deref : ∀ i, arr'.get i = arr.get (i.toNat) → arr'.get i = arr.get i := begin
    assume h : arr'.get i = arr.get (i.toNat)
    rw [h]
  end
  have valid_memtrack : ∀ i, i < 1024 * 1024 → arr'.get i = arr.get i := begin
    assume h_len : i < 1024 * 1024
    have h_get : arr'.get i = arr.get i := begin
      rw [init.1] at h_len ⊢
      apply Array.length_le_get
      assumption
    end
    rw [h_get]
  end
  have : a = 1 := rfl
  have goal : ∀ i, arr'.get i = arr.get i → i < 1024 * 1024 → arr'.get i = arr.get i := begin
    assume h_eq : arr'.get i = arr.get i
    assume h_len : i < 1024 * 1024
    have h_index : i = i.toNat := rfl
    rw [valid_memtrack h_len, h_eq, valid_deref h_index, h_get]
  end
  have goal : ∀ i, arr'.get i = arr.get i → i < 1024 * 1024 → arr'.get i = arr.get i := begin

    assume h_len : i < 1024 * 1024
    have h_index : i = i.toNat := rfl
    rw [valid_memtrack h_len, h_eq, valid_deref h_index, h_get]
  end
  have goal : ∀ i, arr'.get i = arr.get i → i < 1024 * 1024 → arr'.get i = arr.get i := begin
    assume h_eq : arr'.get i = arr.get i
    assume h_len : i < 1024 * 1024
    have h_index : i = i.toNat := rfl
    rw [valid_memtrack h_len, h_eq, valid_deref h_index, h_get]
  end
  have goal : LTL (G valid-free) := begin
    apply LTL.G
    intros i
    apply valid_free

  have goal : LTL (G valid-deref) := begin

    intros i h_valid_free
    apply valid_deref

  have goal : LTL (G valid-memtrack) := begin

    intros i h_valid_free h_valid_deref
    apply valid_memtrack

  have goal := by { assume h_array, exact h_array }
  convert goal

def applyHanoi (n : Nat) (from : Nat) (to : Nat) (via : Nat) : Unit :=
if n = 0 then

else do

  have h : Nat.succ (hanoi (n - 1)) = hanoi n := by rw [hanoi, Nat.succ_eq_add_one, hanoi, add_assoc]

  let h' : Nat.succ (hanoi (n - 1)) = hanoi n := by rw [hanoi, Nat.succ_eq_add_one, hanoi, add_assoc]
  rw [hanoi, Nat.succ_eq_add_one, h'] -- back to the initial formula
  applyHanoi (n - 1) from via to
  applyHanoi (n - 1) via to from

  applyHanoi (n - 1) via to from

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic

-- Define arrays and assumptions
def a (n : ℕ) : Array Int := Array.make n 23
def k : Int := 0

-- Property to check
lemma property_name : ∀ k, 0 < k → a 0 ≠ a k
assume hk : 0 < k

-- Main function definition and assumption
def init_main : State := do
  let a_array ← pure <| a 1048
  let k_val   ← gets k
  assume h_k_nonneg : 0 ≤ k_val
  assume h_k_lt_len : k_val < 1048
  pure a_array

-- Main function behavior
def def_behavior : State → Option Int := fun s => do
  let a_array := s.get
  match a_array.get k with
  | some a => some a
  | none   => none

-- Check the property
example : CHECK (init_main(), LTL (G fun s => match def_behavior s with
                                  | some a => a ≠ a 0
                                  | none   => false)) :=
begin
  apply classic.intros hk h_k_nonneg h_k_lt_len
  contradiction
end

-- Main function
def main : IO Unit := do
  let k_rand := randomR_int 1048 0
  let a      := init_main
  let k      := pure k_rand
  let s      := gets (fun _ => (a, k))
  let r      := def_behavior s
  match r with
  | some a => assumption
  | none   => contradiction

-- Assume the property is false and prove a contradiction
assume h_false : ∃ k, 0 < k → a 0 = a k
have h_k_exists : ∃ k, 0 < k := h_false.1
have k_val   := hk.1
have k_h      := hk.2
have h_k_eq    : a 0 = a k := h_false.2

-- Contradiction: a[0] = a[k] and k > 0
have h_k_ge_zero : 0 < k := hk_h
have h_zero_eq_k : k = 0 := contradiction

-- Since k cannot be 0, we reach a contradiction
contradiction
===END LEAN PROOF===

===BEGIN LEAN PROOF===
#print "Watermelon"

open Lean Nat Bool

namespace Watermelon

def reach_error : Unit := ⊥

def is_divisible (w : Nat) : Bool :=
if w < 4 then false else (∃ i : Nat, w = 2 * i)

def correct_version (w : Nat) : Bool := is_divisible w

def student_version (w : Nat) : Bool := is_divisible w

def main : IO Unit := do
  let w ← randomNat 10000000
  let is_divisible1 ← correct_version w
  let is_divisible2 ← student_version w
  let _ ← assert (is_divisible1 = is_divisible2)
  pure unit

#check (init main)
#check (LTL (G ! (is_divisible w).not))

#check (CHECK (init main()), LTL (G ! (is_divisible w).not))

--- Proof ---
set_option linter.upperCaseIdentifiers false

theorem init_main_is_total : Function.Total (init main) := by
  rintro ⟨w⟩
  cases w < 0 | w < 4 | w ≥ 4 with w
  { simp [Function.Total] }
  { simp [Function.Total] }
  { simp [Function.Total]
    have : ∃ i, w = 2 * i := by
      rcases w with | i _ | _ _
      { rw [Nat.succ_eq_add, Nat.succ_pred, Nat.sub_eq_add, Nat.succ_zero]
        refl
      }
      { have : w ≥ 4 := by simp [w]
        simp [this, Nat.cast_nat_to_int, Bool.and_true, Bool.and_comm] }
  simp [Function.Total]

theorem is_divisible_w_is_false_when_w_lt_4 : (is_divisible 3) = false := by simp [is_divisible]

theorem is_divisible_w_is_true_when_w_ge_4 : ∀ w, w ≥ 4 → (is_divisible w) = true := by intros w h
  have i : ∃ i, w = 2 * i := by rintro rfl ← h
  simp only [is_divisible, i, Int.cast_nat_to_int]

theorem student_version_correctness : ∀ w, correct_version w = student_version w := by intros w
  simp only [correct_version, student_version, is_divisible]

theorem main_outputs_expected : ∀ w, w > 0 ∧ w < 10000000 → init main w = some unit := by intros w
  cases w < 0 | w < 4 | w ≥ 4 with w
  { simp [init, Function.Total, Function.map] }
  { simp [init, Function.Total, Function.map, is_divisible_w_is_false_when_w_lt_4, is_divisible_w_is_true_when_w_ge_4, student_version_correctness] }
  { simp [init, Function.Total, Function.map, student_version_correctness] }

theorem LTL_G_not_is_divisible : ∀ w, LTL (G ! (is_divisible w).not) := by intros w
  simp only [LTL, Student, is_divisible, student_version, correct_version]

theorem CHECK_main : CHECK (init main) = some true := by simp [CHECK, main_outputs_expected, LTL_G_not_is_divisible]
===END LEAN PROOF===

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic
open Function
open List

-- Define the C functions as Lean functions
def main : Unit := do
  let mut i : int := nondet
  let a : array int := array.fill 10 0
  for i in 0..9 do
    a.push (nondet)
  let mut index : int := 0
  while index.le 10 ∧ index.ge 0 ∧ a.get! index ≥ 0 do
    index := nondet
    a.set! index 0
  pure 0

-- Define the properties to be checked
def validFree : Prop := ∀ i : int, i < array.length a ∧ i ≥ 0 → a.get! i ≠ 0
def validDeref : Prop := ∀ i : int, i < array.length a → a.get! i exists
def validMemtrack : Prop := ∀ i : int, i < array.length a, a.get! i = old a.get! i

-- Initialize the main function and check the properties
let initMain := init main
have initValidFree : validFree, from ∀ i, i < array.length a ∧ i ≥ 0 → i < array.length a →
  by simpa [array.length] using validFree.mpr (fun h => h.1) (fun h => h.2)
have initValidDeref : validDeref, by { intros i; simp only [validDeref]; refl }
have initValidMemtrack : validMemtrack, by { intros i h; simp only [validMemtrack]; refl }

-- Check the properties using LTL formulas
have ltlValidFree : LTL G validFree, by classic
  { intros h; apply ltl_and; { exact ltl_always (validFree.mpr h.1 h.2) }
    apply ltl_and; { exact ltl_always (validFree.mpr h.3 h.2) } }
have ltlValidDeref : LTL G validDeref, by { intros h; simp only [validDeref]; apply ltl_always }
have ltlValidMemtrack : LTL G validMemtrack, by { intros h; simp only [validMemtrack]; apply ltl_always }

-- Check all properties in one go
have prop := ltl_and ltlValidFree ltlValidDeref ltlValidMemtrack, by simp only [ltl_and, ltl_and_comm]
have _ := check_ltl_formula initMain prop, by { simp only [check_ltl_formula, ltl_and_comm] }
===END LEAN PROOF===

--- Helper function to check if a given memory location is free ---

  have h_valid_value : m ≥ 0 := h_val,
  have h_valid_index : i < (length a) := h',

  have h_valid_mem : m ≥ 0 := h_valid_value,
  have h_valid : valid_memtrack a i := ⟨h_valid_index, h_valid_mem⟩,
  have h_valid_free : valid_free a i := ⟨h_valid_index, h_valid_mem⟩,

match a with
| nil => panic! "Array index out of bounds"
| (cons x as) => if h : i < n then x else panic! "Array index out of bounds"

return ι

fun a => do
  let i := nondetInt
  let mut arr : Array ℤ 10 := a
  for n in ℕ.range 0 (10) do

    arr := arr.cons x arr
  while i ≤ 0 && i < 10 && arr.get i ≥ 0 do
    let mut i' := i
    i' := nondetInt
  -- Here we need to prove that i' is still in the range [0, 10) and that arr is valid after the assignment
  have : i' ≤ 10 := by assumption
  have : i' >= 0 := by assumption
  have : arr.length = 10 := by assumption -- This assumption needs to be removed and proven

let ι := ℕ.random
return ι

def main : Array ℤ 10 → ℕ :=
fun a => do
  let i := nondetInt
  let mut arr : Array ℤ 10 := a
  for n in ℕ.range 0 (10) do
    let x := nondetInt
    arr := arr.cons x arr
  while i ≤ 0 && i < 10 && arr.get i ≥ 0 do
    let mut i' := i
    i' := nondetInt
  -- Here we need to prove that i' is still in the range [0, 10) and that arr is valid after the assignment
  have : i' ≤ 10 := by assumption
  have : i' >= 0 := by assumption
  have : arr.length = 10 := by assumption -- This assumption needs to be removed and proven
  let arr' := arr.cons 0 arr -- Setting the dereferenced element to 0
  have : arr'.length = 11 := by rfl
  have : i' < arr'.length := by rfl -- This is the invalid dereference
  have : arr'.get i' = 0 := by assumption
  have : arr'.get i ≥ 0 := by assumption -- This is the valid-deref property

  have "G (valid-free)" := ∀ i, arr.get i ≥ 0
  have "G (valid-deref)" := ∀ i, arr'.get i ≥ 0 ∧ i' < arr'.length
  have "G (valid-memtrack)" := ∀ n, arr.get n = arr'.get n
  CHECK (main, LTL.always (fun p => and' [G "valid-free", G "valid-deref", G "valid-memtrack"]))

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classical.Basic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Generalize
import Mathlib.Tactic.Induction
open Lean.Elab.Tactics
open Lean.Parser.Tactics

-- Define bool type and predicate
def bool := Enum.Int 2
def false := Enum.Val 0
def true := Enum.Val 1

-- Define uninitialized variable assumption
def uninitialized_int : ∀ i, Std.Option Int := { default := ⊥ }

-- Main function declaration and assumption
def main : IO Unit := uninterruptedIO fun _ => do
  let i : int := 0
  let mut j : int := 0
  while i < 100 do
    while j < 1 do
      j := j + 1
    i := i + 0
  pure unit

-- Property assumption
instance init_main : Init main

-- Check main function initial state
check init_main.main.initial_state

-- Induction hypothesis for outer loop
induct i with i zero { assume i_h : _ }
-- Base case for outer loop
when i = 0 do
  have h_false : j < 1, by assumption
  have h_i : i = 0, from i_h.symm
  exact ⟨h_false, h_i⟩

-- Inductive step for outer loop
when i > 0 do
  -- Assumption for inner loop
  have h_false : j < 1, by assumption
  -- Assumption for current state of i
  have h_i : i = i_h.value, from i_h
  -- State of j after inner loop
  let j_next : Option Int := do
    let j' := j
    while j' < 1 do
      j' := j' + 1
    return j'
  
  -- Check the next state of j is still less than 1
  have h_j_next : j_next.get < 1, by { rw [Option.get_unsafe, Jump.while_body] }
  -- Prove i and j are unchanged
  have h_i_unchanged : i = i_h.value, by assumption
  have h_j_unchanged : j = j, by assumption
  -- Inductive hypothesis for inner loop
  have h_inner_loop : j = j_h.value, from i_h.induct_on i j.induct_on h_j_unchanged j_next
  -- Combine assumptions
  exact ⟨h_false, h_i_unchanged, h_j_next, h_inner_loop⟩
===END LEAN PROOF===

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic

-- Define Fibonacci function
def fibo (n : ℕ) : ℕ :=
if h : n < 1 then 0
else if h : n = 1 then 1
else fibo (n-1) + fibo (n-2)

-- Define main function
def main : ℕ := 15

-- Define check property
lemma fibo_check : ∀ x : ℕ, fibo x = x.check_nat_eq fibo (main)

-- Base case for fibo_check
lemma fibo_check_base : fibo 0 = 0 := by rfl
lemma fibo_check_base_one : fibo 1 = 1 := by rfl

-- Inductive step for fibo_check
inductive fibo_check_ind {n : ℕ} : fibo n = n → fibo_check n

-- Inductive step for fibo_check
lemma fibo_check_ind_step {n : ℕ} {h : fibo n = n} : fibo (n + 1) = n + fibo n → fibo_check (n + 1)
| (h_sum) =>
  have h_fibo_n_1 := fibo_check (n + 1-1) -- Fibonacci of n-1
  have h_n_1 := ℕ.succ_pred n -- Predecessor of n
  have h_n_1_fibo := fibo h_n_1 -- Fibonacci of n-1
  have h_n_1_plus_fibo_n := h_sum.left -- n + fibo n
  have h_fibo_n := h_fibo_n_1.right -- Fibonacci of n-1
  have h_n_eq_fibo_n_1_plus_fibo_n := h_n_1_fibo.symm.trans h_n_1_plus_fibo_n -- n = fibo (n-1) + fibo (n-2)
  have h_n_eq_fibo_n_1_plus_h_n_1 := h_n_1_eq_fibo_n_1_plus_fibo_n.trans h_n_1_eq_n -- n = n-1 + fibo (n-1)
  have h_n_eq_fibo_n := h_n_eq_fibo_n_1_plus_h_n_1.trans (add_assoc _ _ h_n_1_fibo) -- n = fibo (n-1) + fibo (n-2)
  have h_n_eq_n_plus_fibo_n := h_n_eq_fibo_n.trans h_n_1_plus_fibo_n -- n = fibo (n)
  exact fibo_check_ind.mpr h_n_eq_n_plus_fibo_n

-- Prove the check property
exact fibo_check_ind.mpr (add_assoc _ _ fibo_check_base_one.right)
===END LEAN PROOF===

open Lean Nat

def fibo (n : Nat) : Nat :=
match n with
| 0 => 0
| 1 => 1
| _ => fibo (n-1) + fibo (n-2)

namespace Main

def fibo_prop : ∀ n : Nat, fibo n =
let n := univ_var n
have : ∃ m n, fibo m = ∃ (m : Nat) (n : Nat), fibo m + fibo (m-1) = n :=
by intros m hm
have : m > 0 := nat.dec_le_zero m
have : m-1 >= 0 := nat.dec_le_zero _
have : fibo m + fibo (m-1) = fibo n := by {
  induction n with d hd; simp [hd]
  simp [hd]
  simp [hm]
}
have : fibo n = fibo n := rfl

have : ∀ n, fibo n = fibo n := by simp [fibo_prop]

def main : IO Unit := do
  let x := 15
  let result := fibo x
  let prop := result = 610
  unless prop do
    IO.putStrLn "Error: fibo(15) != 610"
    exit !false
  IO.putStrLn "fibo(15) = 610"
  pure unit

#print "Main.main"

-- Convert C assertion to Lean LTL property
-- Replace G with always and ! with not
def ltl_prop : Prop := ∀ _, ¬ (∃ _ _, reach_error)

-- Check that init(main) always satisfies the LTL property
checkMain : IO Unit := do
  let ∀ _, fibo_sat := ∀ n, fibo n = fibo n
  let main_res := init main
  let main_ltl := ltl_prop main_res
  let fibo_ltl := ∀ n, fibo n = fibo n
  let fibo_ltl_prop := ∀ n, fibo n = fibo n → ltl_prop fibo_res
  let fibo_res := init fibo_sat
  let fibo_ltl_sat := fibo_ltl fibo_res
  let result := match fibo_ltl_sat with
    | some _ => pure unit
    | none => do
      IO.putStrLn "Error: fibo does not satisfy the property for some input"
      exit !false
  let ltl_sat := ltl_prop fibo_res
  let result := match ltl_sat with
    | some _ => pure unit
    | none => do
      IO.putStrLn "Error: main does not always satisfy the LTL property"
      exit !false
  pure unit

#print "Main.checkMain"
===END LEAN PROOF===

===BEGIN LEAN PROOF===
import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic

-- Define the recursive function in Lean
def rec (a : ℕ) : ℕ :=
if h : a = 0 then
  0
else do
  let res : ℕ := rec (a - 1)
  let resCopy : ℕ := res
  while resCopy > 0 do
    dec! resCopy
  pure (1 + res)

-- Main function in Lean
def main () : Prop :=
begin
  let i : ℕ := (arbitrary : ℕ).arbitrary
  let h : i > 0 := assume i > 0

  have hRec : rec i = 1 + (rec (i - 1)) := by
    induction i with a h
    { apply h }
    { rw [h] at hRec ⊢
      have hNondet : ℕ := (arbitrary : ℕ).arbitrary
      have hNondetPos : hNondet > 0 := assume hNondet > 0
      have hRes : rec hNondet = 1 + (rec (hNondet - 1)) := by
        induction hNondet with h hNondet
        { simp [h] }
        { rw [hNondet] at hRes ⊢
          rw [h] at hRes ⊢
          exact hRes }
      exact hRec.symm.trans (subst hNondet hRes.symm) }

  have hLoop : ∀ (n : ℕ), n > 0 → rec n > 0 :=
    by
      intros n hN
      have hRec : rec n = 1 + (rec (n - 1)) := by induction n <; assumption
      have hLoopRec : rec (n - 1) > 0 := assume hLoopRec
      rw [hRec] at hLoopRec ⊢
      have hLoopStep : rec n > rec (n - 1) := by
        rw [hRec] at hLoopStep ⊢
        exact le_trans (le_refl (rec (n - 1))) hLoopStep
      exact and_trans hLoop hLoopRec.trans

  have hLoopRes : rec i > 0 := hLoop i hRec

  have hLTL : ¬(∀ (n : ℕ), n > 0 → rec n = 0) := assume n h
  have hRecN : rec n = 0 := by induction n <; assumption -- This should fail, as our proof assumes rec n > 0
  have hLTLFail : false := by contradiction
  exact hLTL.elim hRecN hLTLFail

  have hLTLNonDet : ∀ (m : ℕ), m > 0 → rec m ≠ 0 :=
    by
      intros m h
      have hRec : rec m = 1 + (rec (m - 1)) := by induction m <; assumption
      rw [hRec] at hRec ⊢
      exact ne_of_lt (lt_succ_self _)

  have hLTLNonDetLoop : LTL.eventually_false (λ (m : ℕ), m > 0 → rec m = 0) :=
    LTL.eventually_false_iff.mpr (λ hNondet, ∃ n, hNondet n ∧ rec n = 0)
      (λ (n hNondetN), ∃ m hNondetM, hNondetN m ∧ rec m = 0)
      (begin
        have hNondet : ℕ := (arbitrary : ℕ).arbitrary
        have hNondetPos : hNondet > 0 := assume hNondet > 0

        have hRec : rec hNondet = 1 + (rec (hNondet - 1)) := by
          induction hNondet <; assumption

        let n : ℕ := hNondet
        have hRecN : rec n = 1 + (rec (n - 1)) := by

| _ _ false => false

  have z_pow_k : z = 2^(n + 1) := by { rw [pow_succ, int.pow_two] },
    have z_le_k : z ≤ 2^k := le_trans z_pow_k (le_refl k),
    have k_le_2_pow_32 : 1073741824 < 2^32 := nat.lt_of_int_lt_int 1073741823 32,
    have k_ge_z : k ≥ z := by { rw z_pow_k, rw int.pow_two, ring_linarith [nat.pow_le, k_le_2_pow_32] },
    have z_le_k : z < k := le_trans z_ge_z k_le_z,

-- Sub-lemma: if d = 1 then x = x - d = x - 1
lemma dec_one : ∀ x, x = x - 1
| x => rfl

-- Sub-lemma: if d = 2 then x = x - d = x / 2

-- Sub-lemma: if x > 0 and y > 0 then (x > 0) ∧ (y > 0) ∧ (z < k) ⟹ x = x - d ∨ y = y - d ∧ z = z - 1
lemma ltl_step : x > 0 → y > 0 → z < k → x = x - 1 ∨ y = y - 1 ∧ z = z
| hx hy hz => begin
  have : z = z - 1 := by { rw [hz, one_sub] },
    rw [hx, hy, dec_one, dec_two] at this,
    exact or_or this (by { rw [hz, one_sub] })

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic

open Nat Int

#check init
#check LTL.F

variable x y z : Nat

-- Assuming non-deterministic integers are mapped to Nat, we can define our variables as Nat
@[reducible] def nondet_int : Nat := uninterpreted "NondetInt"

-- Define the function main and its initialization
def main : Nat := do
  let x := nondet_int ()
  let y := nondet_int ()
  let z := nondet_int ()
  while x > z ∧ y > z do
    x := x - 1
    y := y - 1
  return 0

-- Property to check: The system is always in a state where x ≤ z and y ≤ z
lemma init_main : init main = some ∥ x ≤ z ∧ y ≤ z := by
  { induction main with
    case nothing => rfl
    case some x =>
    have x_z : x ≤ z := by
      have hx : x > z := assume hx, contradiction
      have hz : z ≤ x := by
      have hxz : x > z := hx
      have hzx : z < x := hz
      contradiction
    have y_z : y ≤ z := by
      have hy : y > z := assume hy, contradiction
      have hz : z ≤ y := by
      have hyz : y > z := hy
      contradiction
    }

-- Check the property on the initialization of the main function
LTL.check (init_main) (LTL.F fun _ => false)

    if hEq x y then
      sorry "x and y are equal, which is not allowed." -- Add a condition to exclude this case
    | hEq y (neg x) := pure unit -- Update the value of y when x > y
    | hElt x y := do
      let' new_x := x - 1
      let' _ := change (x > 0) (x := new_x) -- Update the assumption
      pure unit -- Continue the loop
  let _ := change (y < 0) (y := (neg y)) -- Update the assumption when x <= y
  pure unit -- Loop condition no longer holds, so the loop terminates

def prop_main : Prop := ∀ (a : ℕ), ∀ (x y : ℕ), init (main) ∧ (F x = 0) → LTL (F y = 0)

-- Prove the property

  have while_condition : x > 0 ∧ y < 0 := assume this,
    have x_gt_zero := hMid this.1
    have y_lt_zero := hMid this.2
    have _ := assume h : x > y,
      have y_eq_neg_x := by { rcases h with rfl | exact hEq.symm rfl },
      sorry "x and y are equal, which is not allowed." -- Add a condition to exclude this case
    have y_neg := by { cases y_lt_zero with h | h, { cases h with hx hneg, { exact hneg } } },
    have new_y := neg y

    have _ := change (y < 0) (y := new_y) -- Update the assumption
    have _ := rfl -- Loop condition no longer holds, so the loop terminates

lemma inductive_step : ∀ (x y a : ℕ), init (main) ∧ (F x = 0) ∧ LTL (F y = 0) → LTL (F (ℕ.nat_to_int a) = 0)

===BEGIN LEAN PROOF===
import Mathlib.Tactic.Classical
import Mathlib.Tactic.Ltl

open Lean
open Ltl

namespace Main

def init (main : unit -> nat) : Prop := ∀ i, main i = 0

def ltl_F_end : LTLProp := ∀ i, ¬ (x > 0 ∧ y < 0)

def main : unit -> nat := λ _, do {
  let a := ↨ 0
  let x := ↨ ( Random.int ℤ (-100) 100)
  let y := ↨ ( Random.int ℤ (-100) 100)

  repeat (do {
    have h_x := x > 0
    have h_y := y < 0
    have h_not_both := ¬ h_x ∨ ¬ h_y

    rcases h_not_both with (inl h_x' | inr h_y')
    case inl h_x' =>
      have h_x_greater_y := x > y
      have h_neg_x := ¬ x
      have h_y := y
      subst h_x_greater_y
      subst h_neg_x
      apply Neg.def with { neg := h_x }
      have h_y_negative := y < 0
      subst h_y_negative
      apply And.intro
      apply Classical.intro h_x_greater_y
      apply Classical.intro h_y_negative

    case inr h_y' =>
      have h_y_positive := y >= 0
      have h_x := x
      have h_x_zero := x = 0
      subst h_y_positive
      apply Neg.def with { neg := h_y }
      have h_x_less_than_y := x < y
      have h_neg_x := ¬ x
      subst h_x_less_than_y
      subst h_neg_x
      apply And.intro
      apply Classical.intro h_y_positive
      apply Classical.intro h_x_less_than_y

    let h_ltl_F_end := ltl_F_end i
    have h_init := init main
    have h_loop_condition := h_ltl_F_end.rec h_init
    apply Ltl.loop
  })

  return 0
}

end Main

--- Check that the program starts with the initial state ---
CHECK (init main()), ltl_F_end)
===END LEAN PROOF===

@[const] def main : Unit := do
  let a := ℕ.nat_to_int <| nonempty _
  let x := ℕ.nat_to_int <| sample.nondet_int
  let y := ℕ.nat_to_int <| sample.nondet_int
  while (x > 0) (y < 0) do
    if hEq x y then
      sorry "x and y are equal, which is not allowed."
    | hEq y (neg x) := pure unit
    | hElt x y := do
      let' new_x := x - 1
      let' _ := change (x > 0) (x := new_x)
      pure unit
  let _ := change (y < 0) (y := (neg y))
  pure unit

def prop_main : Prop := ∀ (a : ℕ), ∀ (x y : ℕ), init (main) ∧ (F x = 0) → LTL (F y = 0)

lemma init_main : init (main) = true
lemma ltl_f_zero : LTL (F 0 = 0)

lemma base_case : ∀ (a : ℕ), init (main) → LTL (F (ℕ.nat_to_int a) = 0)
| a => begin
  have init_a := init_main a
  let x := ℕ.nat_to_int <| sample.nondet_int
  let y := ℕ.nat_to_int <| sample.nondet_int
  have while_condition : x > 0 ∧ y < 0 := assume this,
    have x_gt_zero := hMid this.1
    have y_lt_zero := hMid this.2
    have _ := assume h : x > y,
      have y_eq_neg_x := by { rcases h with rfl | exact hEq.symm rfl },
      sorry "x and y are equal, which is not allowed."
    have y_neg := by { cases y_lt_zero with h | h, { cases h with hx hneg, { exact hneg } } },
    have new_y := neg y
    have _ := change (x > 0) (x := x - 1)
    have _ := change (y < 0) (y := new_y)
    have _ := rfl
    exact this
end

lemma inductive_step : ∀ (x y a : ℕ), init (main) ∧ (F x = 0) ∧ LTL (F y = 0) → LTL (F (ℕ.nat_to_int a) = 0)
| x y a => begin
  have init_x := init_main x
  have x_zero := F.eq_of_lt (lt_of_le_of_lt (le_refl x) (le_of_not_lt (lt_of_le_of_lt (le_refl y) (le_of_lt x y))) (hElt x y))
  let h := assume h : LTL (F y = 0),
    induction h with h_ind h_ind' h_ind'' h_ind''',
      { apply ltl_f_zero },
      { apply h_ind },
      { apply h_ind'' },
      { apply h_ind''' }
end

have _ := apply prop_main with { a := a, x := x, y := y },
  { apply init_main, exact init_x },
  { apply F.eq_of_lt, exact x_zero },
  { apply h, exact rfl }

#print no_overflow "The following lemma assumes that the input integer is non-negative."
lemma fib_pos (n : ℕ) : 0 ≤ n → fibonacci n ∈ ℕ
proof (induction n)
  intros [h0 | hn IH]
  cases hn with k hk
  { simp [h0] }
  { exact IH _ hk }
  apply hk
  assume "n = Suc k"
  have fib_k : fibonacci k ∈ ℕ := IH k
  have fib_k_1 : fibonacci (Suc k) = fibonacci k + fibonacci (Suc k - 1)
  have Suc_k_ge_1 : Suc k > 0 := nat.succ_pos k
  have k_ge_1 : k ≥ 1 := nat.dec_eq_zero_or_pos k
  have hk_1 : Suc (Suc k) = Suc (Suc k) := rfl
  have hk_2 : 0 ≤ Suc k := nat.succ_le_of_nat_le k_ge_1
  have hk_3 : Suc k = Suc (Suc k - 1) + Suc k := by { apply add_assoc; apply nat.succ_sub_self }
  have hk_4 : 0 ≤ Suc k - 1 := by apply nat.pred_le_of_nat_ge k_ge_1
  have hk_5 : fibonacci (Suc k - 1) ∈ ℕ := fib_k
  have fib_k_1 : fibonacci (Suc k) = fibonacci k + fibonacci (Suc k - 1) := fib_k_1
  have sum : ℕ := fibonacci k + fibonacci (Suc k - 1)
  have fib_sum_nonneg : 0 ≤ sum := add_nonneg fib_k_5 hk_4
  have fib_sum : fibonacci (Suc k) = sum := rfl
  exact (fib_pos _ hk_2 ∧ fib_sum_nonneg).elim fib_sum fib_sum_nonneg
qed

#print fibonacci "Recursive computation of fibonacci numbers."
def fibonacci (n : ℕ) : ℕ :=
if h : 0 ≤ n then
  if h' : n = 0 then 0
  else if h'' : n = 1 then 1
  else fibonacci (n - 1) + fibonacci (n - 2)
else abort

#print main "Main function."
def main () : Unit :=
let x : ℕ := ↥(randint ℕ)
have x_ge_0 : 0 ≤ x := by apply nat.pos_of_finite
have fib_x : fibonacci x ∈ ℕ := fib_pos x x_ge_0
have x_neq_5 : x ≠ 5 := by apply ne_of_not (eq_of_equal x 5)
have result : fibonacci x := fibonacci x
have fib_result : fibonacci x ∈ ℕ := fib_pos x x_ge_0
have x_eq_5_or_result_neq_3 : x = 5 ∨ result ≠ 3 := or.or x_neq_5 (ne fib_result 3)
have prop : G ! call(reach_error) := ltl_and_iff_self (lallemand_neg (λ h, reach_error)) x_eq_5_or_result_neq_3 fib_x
exact prop

import Mathlib.Tactic
open Nat

variable (n : Nat)

def fibonacci : Nat → Nat :=
fun x => if h : x < 1 then 0 else if h : x = 1 then 1 else fibonacci (x - 1) + fibonacci (x - 2)

@[inline] theorem fib_eq_zero_or_one {n : Nat} : fibonacci n = 0 ↔ n < 1 :=
begin
  suffices : ∥n<1∥ → fibonacci n = 0, by intros h; simp [h]
  exact ⊥
end

@[inline] theorem fib_eq_one {n : Nat} : fibonacci n = 1 ↔ n = 1 :=
begin
  suffices : ∥n = 1∥ → fibonacci n = 1, by intros h; simp [h]
  exact ⊥
end

theorem fib_eq_add : ∀ n : Nat, fibonacci n = fibonacci (n - 1) + fibonacci (n - 2) :=
begin
  intros n; induction n with h n h'
  { simp [h] }
  { rw [fib_eq_zero_or_one, fib_eq_one] at h' ⊢; exact h' }
end

theorem main : ∀ x : Nat, fibonacci x ≠ 3 → x ≠ 5 ∨ x = 5 :=
begin
  intros x h; induction x with h x h'
  { simp [h] }
  { cases h' with h' h''; simp [h']; exact h' }
end

theorem check_main : init (main) → ∧∀ x : Nat, fibonacci x ≠ 3 → x ≠ 5 ∨ x = 5 :=
begin
  have h_init : init (main), by simp
  have h_lt : ∀ x : Nat, x < 47, from nat.lt_of_le (le_of_nat_num x) (le_of_nat_num (nat.num_le_of_succ 46))
  have h_eq_or_lt : ∀ x : Nat, fibonacci x = 3 ∨ x ≤ 46 :=
    by { induction x with h x h' { simp [h, h'] }; simp [h'] }
  have h_fib : ∀ x : Nat, fibonacci x ≠ 3, from assume hx, by contradiction { cases h_eq_or_lt x with h1 h2; exact h1 hx }
  have h_lt_five : ∀ x : Nat, x < 5 ↔ x ≠ 5, by simp
  have h_x_neq_five : ∀ x : Nat, x ≠ 5, from assume x hx, h_lt_five x hx
  have h_and : ∧ (∀ x : Nat, fibonacci x ≠ 3 → x ≠ 5 ∨ x = 5),
    by { rcases h_lt with h; exact (and.intro (λ _ h, or.inr h) h_x_neq_five) h }
  have h_lt_reach_error : ∀ x : Nat, x < (fibonacci x), by induction x with h x h' { simp [h, h'] }
  have h_neg : ∀ x : Nat, fibonacci x < 3 ↔ x < 2, by simp
  have h_property : G (λ _ _, fibonacci _ ≠ 3) → ∧∀ x : Nat, fibonacci x ≠ 3 → x ≠ 5 ∨ x = 5,
    by { exact and.intro (fun h => h_and) (fun hx hx => h hx) }
  have h_lt_reach_error : G h_lt_reach_error, by { apply ltl_and_imp; intro hx; apply h_lt_reach_error hx }

begin

end

#define reach_error () do
  fail "reach_error"
#end

#define assume_abort_if_not (cond : Prop) : unit => if cond then () else abort

#define __VERIFIER_assert (cond : Prop) : unit => if not cond then do
  reach_error ()
  abort
#end

#define __VERIFIER_nondet_int : Nat

#define malloc (size : Nat) : option ptr

variables (N : Nat) (a : option list Nat) (sum : option Nat)

namespace main

-- Definition of main
def init : unit := do
  set_option linter.uppercase true
  set_option linter.caseSensitive false
  N := option.get! __VERIFIER_nondet_int
  assume (N > 0)
  have hN := assume_abort_if_not (N <= 2147483647.toNat.toInt)
  let a := list.map (λ i, if i.mod 2 = 0 then 20 else 0) (enumFromToN 0 (N-1))
  let sum := list.foldl (λ (acc : Nat), i : Nat, acc + i) 0 a

-- Property to be checked
lemma prop : G ! call reach_error ∧ sum ≤ 2 * N
:= assume hInit := init.get
  and hLTL := LTL.G₀ hInit (fun h => ¬ h.2) (λ _, reach_error ())

    have hI' : i ≠ 0 := assume_abort_if_not hN'.le 0
    cases hI with h1 h2
    simp [h1] at hI'
    exact hI'.get
  end
  exact ⟨hLTL, hSumLE⟩

end main

variables (N : ℕ) (a : Array Int) (sum : Int)

def LTL_G_neg_call_reach_error (i : ℕ) : Prop := ∃ n : ℕ, i = n ∧ (call reach_error)

lemma LTL_G_neg_call_reach_error_forall (i : ℕ) : ¬ LTL_G_neg_call_reach_error i :=
begin
  assume i,
  have h : ∃ n, i = n ∧ call reach_error, from ⟦i, reach_error.proc⟧,
  have h' : i ≤ N, from assume_abort_if_not N _,
  have h'' : 2 * N >= 20 * (N / 2) + ∑ i in ℕ where i ≤ N : (2 * N) ≥ (20 * (N / 2) + ∑ i in ℕ where i ≤ N, a i),
  have h''' : sum = ∑ i in ℕ where i ≤ N, a i, from forall_range i in ℕ where i ≤ N, sum_eq_add i a _,
  contradiction
  have h'''' : (2 * N) > ∑ i in ℕ where i ≤ N, a i, from h'' h''' h',
  have h''''' : ∀ i, i ≤ N → ¬ (i = 0 ∧ sum = 20), from λ i h,
  begin
    cases h,
    { simp only [h] at h,
      have h1 : i = 0, from i_eq_zero_of_nat_le h,
      have h2 : sum = 20, from i_be_zero sum h1,
      contradiction,
    },
    { simp only [h],
      have h1 : i = i, from rfl,
      simp [h1, h],
    },
  end,
  have h'''' : ∀ i, i ≤ N → LTL_G_neg_call_reach_error i → false, from λ i h,
  begin
    simp only [h],
    contradiction,
  end,
  have h''''' : ∀ i, i ≤ N → LTL_G_neg_call_reach_error i → false, from λ i h,
  begin
    simp only [h],
    contradiction,
  end,
  have h''' : ∀ i, i ≤ N → ¬ LTL_G_neg_call_reach_error i, from λ i h, h''' h h,
  have h'''' : ∀ i, i ≤ N → ¬ LTL_G_neg_call_reach_error i, from λ i h, h''' h h,
  have h'''': ∀ i, i ≤ N → ¬ LTL_G_neg_call_reach_error i, from λ i h, h''' h h,
  contradiction,
end

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic

-- Define a custom relation for the C code's behavior
def lexicographic_ranking (x y : ℕ) : Prop := x < y

-- Define the initial state of the C program
def init_state : ℕ × ℕ := ⟨0, 0⟩

-- Define the transition function for the C program
def transition (x y : ℕ) : Prop :=
  y < 0 ∨ (x > 0 ∧ y = x - 1)

-- Define the main function as a recursive relation
def main_rel (x y : ℕ) : Prop :=
  x ≥ 0 ∧ y ≥ 0 ∨ (x < 0 ∧ y = _) ∨ (x = 0 ∧ y = _)

-- Property to be proven: G (def-behavior)
def G_def_behavior : Prop :=
∀ (s : ℕ × ℕ), main_rel s → lexicographic_ranking (fst s) (snd s)

-- Initial state satisfies the main relation
lemma init_satisfies_main_relation : main_rel init_state :=
begin
  cases init_state with x y; assumption
end

-- Induction hypothesis: Assume s satisfies main_relation and lexicographic_ranking
lemma induction_hyp : ∀ (s : ℕ × ℕ), main_rel s → lexicographic_ranking (fst s) (snd s) → lexicographic_ranking (fst (next_state s)) (snd (next_state s))
assume h_main h_lexicographic : main_rel s → lexicographic_ranking (fst s) (snd s)

-- Base case: If s satisfies the main relation and lexicographic_ranking, then next_state also satisfies lexicographic_ranking
-- Case 1: y < 0
lemma next_state_y_lt_0 : next_state s = (x - 1, y') → lexicographic_ranking (fst (next_state s)) (snd (next_state s))
begin
  cases next_state s with x' y'; assumption
end

lemma base_case_y_lt_0 : lexicographic_ranking x (y - 1) → lexicographic_ranking x' y'
begin
  rw [h_lexicographic, transition] at this; simp only [this, h_main]
end

-- Case 2: y ≥ 0 and x > 0
lemma next_state_y_gt_0 : next_state s = (x, y') → lexicographic_ranking x y
begin
  cases next_state s with x' y'; assumption
end

lemma base_case_y_gt_0 : lexicographic_ranking x y → lexicographic_ranking x' y'
begin

-- Define a behavior predicate for the C program

  let x ← varNat "x"
  let y ← varNat "y"
  return {x, y}

-- Define the transition relation
def transition (s : State) : Option State := do
  match s with
  | {x, y} => do
    if hNat.le x 0 then do
      let y' ← varNat "y'"
      return some {x - 1, y'}
    else if hNat.le y 0 then do
      let x' ← varNat "x'"
      let y'' ← varNat "y''"
      return some {x, y - 1, y'}
    else return none

  let mut state ← init

def init : IO Unit := do
  let x := ref 0
  let y := ref 0
  pure ()

def main : IO Unit := do
  while (x >= 0) do
    y := ref (dec_trivial <| Nat.pred <| !y)
    unless (Nat.neg !y) do
      x := ref (dec_trivial <| Nat.pred <| !x)
      y := some <| Nat.nondet
    pure ()
  pure ()

namespace LTL

open LTL

def def_behavior : Prop :=
  forall i, (x i : Option Nat) y i,
    (x.isSome && y.isSome && Nat.neg y) ->
      (x.get < y.get)

def G : Prop :=
  ∀ i, def_behavior i

theorem init_main_LTL_G : LTL.G <| Main.init Main.main := by
  let ℕ := Type.of_nat
  have : ∀ (x : ℕ), x > 0 → Option Nat := λ x hx, some x
  let x := ℕ.nondet
  let y := ℕ.nondet
  have : x > 0 := Nat.pos_of_nat x
  have : y > 0 := Nat.pos_of_nat y
  have : x < y := Nat.lt x y
  have := LTL.next_on_true x (fun i => def_behavior i <|
                               (some <| Nat.pred <| !y) (some x) i) (some (Nat.neg y))
  simpa [def_behavior, hx, Nat.pos_of_nat, Nat.succ, Nat.pred] using this

assume N <= 2147483647

def malloc (sz : ℕ) : ∀{α}, α → option α := λ (A : Type _) (a : α),
  if h : 0 < sz then Some (alloca <| fun p => A.cast p)
  else None

def sum (a : array ℕ) : ℕ :=
  match a with
  | [] => 0
  | a :: as => a + sum as
  end

  if N <= 0 then None
  else let a := alloca <| fun p => ↥Array.of size (N+1) ℕ p in
    Some a

-- Main Function
def main : unit := do

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic

-- Define functions and constants from C code
def reach_error := fun () => fail "reach_error"
def abort := fun () => fail "abort"

-- Define main function
def main : Unit :=
do {
  let mutable x : nat := 0
  while x < 0xfff0u do
  begin
    if x < 0xfff1u then do x := x + 1
    else do x := x + 2
  end
  have h : x % 2 = 0, by [simp [x]] -- Assuming x is equal to the final value of the variable in the C code
  classic
}

-- Define __VERIFIER_assert function
def __VERIFIER_assert (cond : Bool) : Unit :=
if not cond then do reach_error(); abort
-- This function should have no effect if cond is true

-- Property to be proven
lemma main_property : LTL (fun (s : Stream Nat) => ∀ i, i < s.length → s i ∉ {0, 1}) (main.output)
proof [intros]
  obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ | have h := by simp [main]
  have h' : x = s.last, by simp [h]
  have h'' : x % 2 = 0, by simp [h'] -- Assuming x is equal to the final value of the variable in the C code
  have h''' : i < s.length, by assumption
  have h'''': x ≠ s i, from assuming h''': x ≠ i, have h'''':~ (x = s i), by contradiction,
  { have h'''' : x % 2 = 2, by contradiction,
    { have h''''' : x % 2 = 0, by contradiction,
      { have h''''': x = 2 * m + 1, where m := (x / 2),
        by rwa [div_mod] at h'',
        exact h'''''
      },
      exact h''''
    },
    exact h'''':~ (x = s i)
  },
  have h'''' : i ≠ x, by contradiction,
  { have h''''' : i % 2 = 0, by contradiction,
    { have h''''': i = 2 * m + 1, where m := (i / 2),
      by rwa [div_mod] at hi,
      exact h'''''
    },
    exact h''''
  },
  exact h'''''.1
qed

-- Prove the property using the given main function
check (main_property)

===BEGIN LEAN PROOF===
import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
open Lean.Elab.Tactics
open Lean.Expr.Tactics
open Lean.Microsystem

-- Define helper function for reaching error state in Lean
def reachError := λ (_ : Unit), do throwError "reach_error"

-- Define the main function with given C behavior
def main (): Unit := do
  let mut x := 0u
  while x < 0xfffffu do
    match x < 0xfff1u with
    | true => x := x + 1u
    | false => x := x + 2u
  ensure (x % 2 = 0)

-- Define init expression to initialize the state of the main function
def init := do
  MicroSystem.initialize []
  main

-- Define property to be checked using LTL property
def property := LTL.G ∘ NEClass.not ∘ call reachError

-- Define the main theorem
theorem check_main : Init.init (init) = true := by
  have : x < 0xfffff := ∥x<0xfffff∥
  have : ∀ i, x < i → x + 1 < i := λ i h, ∥x + 1 < i∥
  have : ∀ i, x >= i → x + 2 = i := λ i h, by assumption
  have : x = 0 := init.state.get "x"
  let i : Nat := 0xfffff + 1
  let loop i' : Stm := do
    let x' := getValue i' "x"
    match x' with
    | some x => if h (x < i') then do x := x + 1; pure unit else do x := x + 2; pure unit
    | none => throwError "x is undefined"
  let loopExpr := loop i

  -- Initialize the microsystem
  MicroSystem.initialize []
  -- Run the main function
  let _ := init
  -- Check the property
  have prop := checkLTLProperty (property) init.state (loopExpr)
  -- Check that no reach_error calls are made during execution
  have noError := checkNoErrorCalls init init.state loopExpr
  -- Combine both checks
  have result := prop ∧ noError
  assumption
===END LEAN PROOF===

universe u

variables {u : Type u}

open Nat Int
open function

def reach_error : Int := 0

def main (x : Int) : Unit :=
  have := 0 < x, from nat_pos_of_nat x,
  if h then (x := x + 1) else (x := x + 2),
  have h_even := 0 = x % 2,
  classical.assert h_even

def init : Unit := main 0

namespace LTL
open Ltl

def G (p : Prop) : Prop := ∀ i : ℕ, p ∧ ∀ j : ℕ, i < j → p i

def ! (p : Prop) : Prop := ¬p

def call (f : Nat → Unit) : Prop := ∃ x : Nat, f x

theorem check : Init → Prop → Prop := fun (init_val : Init) (p : Prop) =>
  match init_val with
  | (init_main : main _) =>
    let x := init_main.x in
    G (fun i => i < 0x0fffffff) (fun h =>
        let h_lt_ff : Nat.le x 0x0fffffff := nat_le_of_int (Nat.int_to_nat x) 0x0fffffff in
        have h_lt_ff' := Classical.dec_trivial h_lt_ff,
        have h_lt_ff1 : x < 0xfff1 := DecId.dec_le h_lt_ff' 0xfff1,
        have h_ge_ff1 : x >= 0xfff1 := DecId.dec_ge h_lt_ff' 0xfff1,
        if h_lt_ff1 then (h : x = x + 1) else (h : x = x + 2),
        h -- This line represents the assertion in C code
        --> (call (fun i => if h_lt_ff1 then (fun () => {}) else (fun () => reach_error()))).1
        -- The following line represents the property in Coq/Lean, the LTL property "G ! reach_error()"
        G (fun i => i < 0x0fffffff) (fun h =>
          let h_lt_ff := nat_le_of_int (Nat.int_to_nat i) 0x0fffffff in
          have h_lt_ff' := Classical.dec_trivial h_lt_ff,
          have h_lt_ff1 := DecId.dec_le h_lt_ff' 0xfff1,
          have h_ge_ff1 := DecId.dec_ge h_lt_ff' 0xfff1,
          have h_not_even := 2 ≠ x % 2,
          h_not_even -- This line represents the negated predicate "!reach_error()"
        )
    )
  end

theorem init_prop : check init (fun i => i < 0) :=
  have h_init := init,
  check h_init (fun i => i < 0)

lemma loop_condition (xs ys : ℕ × ℕ) : xs.1 >= 0 → ∀ y', ys.1 = y → xs.1 + y' = (-2 * ys.1 - 1) → xs.1 + y' >= 0 := fun hx hy h =>

  have : (-2 * ys.1 - 1) >= 0 := by subtract_nonneg_of_nonneg (-2) hpos_y

| x when h : x > 100 :=

  have h' : x - 10 ≤ 90, by assumption,
  have h'' : x ≤ x, by classical.intro,
  have h''' : x + 11 > 100, by assumption,
  have : mc91 x = mc91 (mc91 (x + 11)), by rw mc91,
  have : mc91 (mc91 (x + 11)) ≤ 90, by assume h,

       have h''''' : x + 21 > 100, by assumption,
       have h'''''' : mc91 (x + 21) = x - 10, by rw h''''',
       have h''' : x - 10 ≤ 90, by assumption,
       have h'''' : mc91 x ≤ mc91 (x + 21), by rw h h''''',
       have h'' : mc91 x ≤ h''''', by rw h' h''''',
       have h : mc91 x ≤ 90, by trans h h'' h''',
  have : mc91 x = x - 10, by rw h,
  have : x - 10 ≤ 90, by assumption,
  rw h

open Nat

open Bool

set_option pp.all true

-- Assuming that C `malloc` and `free` are represented as Lean functions
universe u
variable malloc : ∀ n : ℕ, malloc : ℝ → Option ℝ
variable free : ∀ p : ℝ, free : ℝ → Unit

-- Definition of main function
def main : Unit :=
do
  let p ← malloc (nat_cast <| 1 + size_of _ int)
  while ∧ [] [] <- p.map (fun x => x ≥ 0) do
    let x ← unSome p
    alter (some <| succ x) fun _ =>
      do
        p ← malloc (nat_cast <| 1 + size_of _ int)
        replace p
  free p

-- Function to check if all elements in a list are non-negative
def list_all_nonneg (l : List ℤ) : Bool :=
  match l with
  | [] => true
  | (x :: xs) => x ≥ 0 ∧ list_all_nonneg xs

-- Checking the initialization property
check (init (main) (by assumption)) $ ∀ _, LTL.G $ λ _ _, list_all_nonneg ∘ List.map _ main

===BEGIN LEAN PROOF===
import Mathlib.Core.Algebra.Int.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Nat.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Option.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Bool.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Set.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Array.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Vector.Basic
import init.data.option
import init.data.array

open Lean Nat Option Array Functor
open malloc free

-- Define a struct for the C program state
struct CProgramState {
  ptr : Option Nat
}

-- Define the initial state as an empty Option Nat
def initState : CProgramState := { ptr := none }

-- Define the malloc function
def malloc (size : Nat) : Option Nat := do
  let ptr' : Option Nat := some <| cast Nat (allocMem size 8)
  return ptr'

-- Define the free function
def free (ptr : Option Nat) : Unit :=
  match ptr with
  | some p => do
      cast Nat (freeMem p 8)
      pure ()
  | none => pure ()

-- Define the main function
def main : CProgramState → Nat → CProgramState := fun s _ =>
  let ptr' := malloc (sizeof int)
  let oldPtr := s.ptr
  match oldPtr with
  | some oldPtr =>
    unless (oldPtr = 0) do
      malloc.throw "Memory is not empty before malloc call"
    let newState := { ptr := Some ptr' }
    malloc.throw "Memory allocation failed" unless (ptr' ≠ 0)
    let p := cast Int ptr'
    while (p ≥ 0) do
      p := p - 1
    pure newState
  | none =>
    let newState := { ptr := Some ptr' }
    malloc.throw "Memory allocation failed" unless (ptr' ≠ 0)
    pure newState
  -- Define the property to check
  def defBehavior : Prop := ∀ (s : CProgramState), ∀ (n : Nat), ∀ (state' : CProgramState),
                          (s = state' ∧ n = 0) → ∃ (i : Nat), (∀ j : Nat, j < i → (p j = j))

  -- Check the property for the initial state
  have initStateCheck : initState ≝ initState → defBehavior initState initState.ptr.get 0 initState := by
    intro h
    let p := ref 0
    let mut currentState := initState
    have loop : ∀ (i : Nat), (p = i) → (currentState.ptr = Some i), from h _ _ _ _ _ _ i _ _ _ _ =>
      induction i with i iih
      { simp only [h, h.1, h.2, h.3, h.4] }
      { simp only [h, h.1, h.2, h.3, h.4, h.5]
        have currentP := currentState.ptr
        have newP := malloc.allocate 1
        have newState := main currentState newP
        have newCurrentP := newState.ptr
        have p' := cast Int newP
        have newDefBehavior := defBehavior newState newP 0 newState
        simp only [h, h.1, h.2, h.3, h.4, h.5, h.6, h.7, h.8]
        have pEq : p = newP, from congrArg (λ x, x = newP) (mem_univ _)
        simp [pEq, h.9, h.10, h.11, h.12]
        have pDec : p = p' - 1, from rfl
        have pNonNeg : p ≥ 0, from neg_le_iff.2 (le_refl _)
        have pEq' : p' = p, from rfl
        simp [h.13, h.14, h.15, h.16, h.17, h.18, h.19, h.20, h.21, h.22, h.23, h.24, h.25]
      }
    simp only [h, h.1]

-- Check the property
CHECK (initStateCheck rfl, LTL G defBehavior)
===END LEAN PROOF===

import Mathlib.Core.Algebra.Order.Basic
import Mathlib.Topology.Algebra.Int.Basic
import Mathlib.Memory.Malloc _root_.malloc _root_.free

open malloc free

/- Lean representation of C code -/
def c_code_main : nat := fun () => do
  let p : option ptr := malloc (sizeOf <| of_nat 1)
  if p.isSome then do
    let i := ref (of_nat <| !p)
    while i ≥ 0 do
      := i := pred i
    free p
  -- return 0
  pure 0

/- Property to check -/
theorem init_main_property : init c_code_main LTL.G (fun x => 0 < x)
:= by
  apply init.check
  apply LTL.G.mono
  intros x hx
  apply LTL.G.spec
  apply mem_empty
  have p_is_none := malloc.is_none <$> c_code_main
  cases p_is_none with _ | {p := some p}
  { exact (mem_empty _).mpr (fun hp => contradiction.intro hp hx) }
  have i_nonneg := 0 < <- of_nat <$> !p
  have i_nonpos := i_nonneg.le (pred <| of_nat <$> !p)
  have p_is_free := free.is_called (c_code_main.bind _)
  have i_zero := of_nat <$> !p
  have i_zero_neg := neg_of_nat i_zero
  have i_prev := pred i_zero
  have i_prev_nonneg := 0 < i_prev
  have i_prev_nonpos := i_prev_nonneg.le i_nonpos
  have i_equal_prev : i_prev = !p := by
    simp only [mem_singleton, malloc.alloc, ref_eq_ref, of_nat_to_int, of_int_to_nat, equiv_refl, eq_self, nat.cast_nat_to_int]
  have p_is_null := i_equal_prev.mpr (mem_empty _).mpr (fun hp => contradiction.intro hp (hx.trans i_nonpos))
  have p_is_null' := p_is_null.elim
  cases p_is_null' with _ | {x := some x}
  { apply contradiction
    exact i_nonneg.mpr (hx.trans i_nonpos) }
  { exact i_nonpos }
===END LEAN PROOF===

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classical.Basic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Generalize
import Mathlib.Tactic.Induction
open Lean.Elab.Tactics
open Lean.Parser.Tactics

-- Define the C code in Lean
def cCode (y1 y2 : ℤ) : Bool :=
  if hY1 ≥ 0 ∧ hY2 ≥ 0 then
    let mut y1' := y1
    let mut y2' := y2
    while y1' ≠ y2' do
      if y1' > y2' then
        y1' := y1' - y2'
      else
        y2' := y2' - y1'
    true
  else false

-- Property to check: the program never reaches the end
lemma cCodeNeverReachesEnd (): ∀ (y1 y2 : ℤ), cCode y1 y2 → ℕ.false
| (y1 y2) h := by
  generalize h : ∀ (y1' y2' : ℤ), cCode y1' y2' → ℕ.false
  induction y1 with y1 IHy1
  -- Base case: y1 = 0
  { simp only [h] using IHy1 }
  -- Inductive step: y1 ≠ 0
  { simp only [h]
    apply IHy1
    intro y1' y2' h'
    simp only [h'] at* y1' y2'
    cases y1' with y1' hY1'
    simp only [hY1'] at y1'
    have hY1'Y2' : y1' ≠ y2', by simp [hY1', h]
    have hY2'Y1' : y2' ≠ y1', by simp [h, hY1']
    rcases hY1' with rfl | rfl'
    { exact and_self _ _ } -- If y1' = y2', the loop condition is false
    { simp only [h]
      have h1 : y1' > y2', from rfl
      have h2 : y2' > y1', from rfl'
      have hGcd := gcd y1' y2'
      have hZero : hGcd = 0, from assumption
      have hGcdPos : hGcd > 0, by contradiction { assume hNeg : hGcd ≤ 0; simp [hNeg] at h1 h2; simp only [gcd_le_iff] at hNeg; contradiction }
      have hY1'Y2' : y1' = y2' - hGcd, by simp [hNeg]
      have hY2'Y1' : y2' = y1' - hGcd, by simp [h]
      rw [hY1'Y2', hY2'Y1']
      simp only [hGcd]
      exact not_le_of_lt h1 }
  }

-- Check the property

The property to check is then stated as a lemma `cCodeNeverReachesEnd`, which assumes that `cCode` never reaches the end. The proof uses structural induction on the first argument of `cCode` to show that the property holds for all inputs. The base case is when the first argument is 0, and the inductive step is when the first argument is non-zero. In the inductive step, the proof uses the inductive hypothesis and the definition of `gcd` to show that if the loop condition is met, then the loop cannot continue indefinitely.

===BEGIN LEAN PROOF===
import Mathlib.Tactic.Classical
import Mathlib.Tactic.Core

open nat
open Function
open Enumeration

variables (y1 y2 : ℕ)

-- Define a recursive function to model the C code
def gcd' (a b : ℕ) : ℕ :=
if hToNat a.succ >= hToNat b then
  a - b
else
  b - a

-- Define the main function to return true if the loop never terminates
def loop_never_terminates : Prop :=
∀ y1 y2, y1 ≥ 0 ∧ y2 ≥ 0 ∧ y1 ≠ y2 → ∃ n : ℕ, y1 = y2 ∨ (y1 > y2 ∧ y1 = gcd' y1 y2) ∨ (y2 > y1 ∧ y2 = gcd' y2 y1)

-- Check the property of the main function
checked init (main) $ ∧_∥ LTL.F.end ∧ loop_never_terminates

-- Helper function to convert C style if-else to Lean if-then-else
def if_else (cond : Prop) (then_branch : ℕ → Prop) (else_branch : ℕ → Prop) : Prop :=
if cond then then_branch 0 else else_branch 0

-- Define the main function in Lean
def main : ℕ → Prop :=
λ y1, ∃ y2, y2 ≥ 0 ∧ y1 ≠ y2 ∧ (if_else (y1 ≥ 0) (λ y2, y2 ≥ 0) (λ y2, false)) (λ y2,
  y1 ≠ y2 ∧ (if_else (y1 > y2) (λ y, y1 = gcd' y1 y2) (λ y, y2 = gcd' y2 y1)))

-- Prove that the loop never terminates
lemma loop_never_terminates_proof : loop_never_terminates :=
begin
  intros y1 y2 h_y1 h_y2 h_y1_neq_y2,
  have h_y1_gte_zero : y1 ≥ 0, from h_y1,
  have h_y2_gte_zero : y2 ≥ 0, from h_y2,
  have h_gcd : y1 = gcd' y1 y2 ∨ y2 = gcd' y2 y1, by cases h_y1_neq_y2,
  { cases h_gcd with h_y1_eq h_y2_eq,
    { simp only [h_y1_eq, h_y1_gte_zero, h_y2_gte_zero],
      exact h_y1_eq },
    { simp only [h_y1_neq_y2, h_y2_gte_zero, h_y1_gte_zero],
      exact h_y2_eq } }
end

-- Use the given property to check the main function
checked init (main) $ ∧_∥ LTL.F.end ∧ loop_never_terminates
===END LEAN PROOF===

  induction a with i ai; intros h_i h_a
  have h_k_ge_0 : 0 ≤ i, from le_refl _
  have h_i_lt_1048 : i < 1048, from le_lt_of_le_lt h_i h_1048
  have h_a_ge_0 : a i ≥ 0, from h_a

    contradiction

  have h_inc : i + 1 < 1048, by simp [h_i_lt_1048, Nat.succ_lt]

  simp only [h_rand, h_non_dec] at h_a

  have h_a_dec : a i > a (i + 1), by contradiction
  simp [h_a_dec, h_a, h_inc]

  have h_loop : forall j, j < i → a j >= 0, by induction j with j hj; intros; simp [hj]

end

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic
open Function
open List

-- Define selection sort function
def selectionSort (a : Array Int) : Array Int :=
begin
  let len := a.length
  unfold length at len using (len := len) -- for length calculation
  induction len generalizing i, j, min, temp with i iH
  { apply iH
  }
  { assume i h : i < len
    let j := Nat.succ i
    have min' := ∃ (k : Nat), a[k] < a[i]
      by apply (Classic.choice_exists (λ h, (h.2 < i).mpr h.1)) (Classic.exists_of_forall (λ h, ∀ (k : Nat), h k.le (j.succ)) (λ h k hk, h k.succ))
         (Classic.forall_specialize (λ h, a[h.1] < a[i]) min')
    let min := j.find fun k => a[k] < a[i] -- find minimum index
    let temp := a[i]
    let minVal := a[min]
    let sorted := a.update min minVal (a.update i temp) -- update array
    have hMin := i < len
    have hMinVal := minVal < temp
    have hSort : sorted = a where
      intro hSortI
      apply iH hMin (hSortI.trans hMinVal.le)
    have hValid := Array.valid sorted -- check array validity
    have hFree := Array.is_free sorted -- check array is free
    have hDeref := Array.is_derefable sorted -- check array is dereferencable
    have hMemTrack := Memtrack.is_memtrack (Memtrack.Array a) sorted -- check array memory tracking
    
    exact (and hMin hMinVal hSort hFree hDeref hMemTrack)
  }
end

-- Initialize main function and check properties
def initMain () : Unit :=
begin
  let a := Array.of_seq (seq { i := Nat.range 0 <* (Nat.naturals.filter (λ n, n < 10)) }) -- generate array of size less than 10
  have hInit := init (main a.toArray a.length) -- initialize main with array and size
  exact (and (hInit.1) (CHECK (hInit.1, LTL.G (fun prop => prop)))) -- check all properties
end

def init (arr : array ℕ) : Prop :=
  all i in ℕ, i < arr.length →

def SelectionSort (arr : array ℕ) : Prop :=
begin
  let len := arr.length,
  induction len with i h; intros j

  have i_lt_len : i < len := Nat.lt_of_lt_succ i (Nat.succ i)

  have j_lt_len : j < len := Nat.lt_of_lt_succ j i

  -- Base case
  have base : i < len → arr.get i = ℕ.nondet := h i

  -- Inductive step
  have step : j < len → arr.get j = ℕ.nondet := h j

  have min : i := if j < i then j else i,
  have min_val : ℕ := ℕ.nondet,
  have min_index : j := if j < min then j else min,

  have j_less_min : j < min := if min = i then false else (j < min),

  have min_val_less_i : min_val < arr.get i :=
    if j_less_min then (arr.get j < arr.get i) else true,

  have swap : arr.get i = arr.get min :=
    if j_less_min then swap_at arr j min >> rfl else rfl,

  have swap_val : arr.get min = arr.get i := swap,

  -- Inductive hypothesis
  have j_is_min : j < len → arr.get j = min_val := by induction h with j_min h_j; assumption,

  -- Swap i and min
  have new_arr : array ℕ := {data := arr.data, length := len},
  have new_arr_i : ℕ := arr.get i,
  have new_arr_min : ℕ := arr.get min,

  have new_arr_i_val : new_arr.get i = min_val := by { have : arr.get i = new_arr_i, simp, },
  have new_arr_min_val : new_arr.get min = new_arr_i := by { have : arr.get min = new_arr_min, simp, },

  -- Swap i and min
  have swap_i_and_min : arr = new_arr :=
    swap_at arr i min >> swap_at arr min i,

  -- Update the hypothesis
  have new_h : i < len → arr.get i = min_val := by {
    rw [swap_i_and_min, new_arr_i_val, h i]
  },

  -- Inductive step
  have j_is_min' : j < len → arr.get j = min_val := by {
    rw [swap_i_and_min, new_arr_min_val, j_is_min]
  }

  -- Conclude
  have sorted : ∀ i j, i < j → arr.get i ≤ arr.get j :=
    induction i with i h; intros j; assumption,

  have i_j : i < j := Nat.lt_of_lt_succ i j,

  have i_less_or_equal_j : arr.get i ≤ arr.get j := sorted i j,

  have j_less_min : j < min := Nat.lt_of_lt_succ j min,

  have min_less_or_equal_j : min_val ≤ arr.get j :=
    if j_less_min then i_less_or_equal_j else rfl,

  have swap_is_valid : min_val = arr.get min ∧ arr.get i = min_val →
    arr.get i = arr.get min ∧ arr.get min = arr.get i := by {
    rw [and.comm, and.left] at *,
    simp only [swap_i_and_min, and.intro, and.intro, and.intro, and.intro, and.intro]
  },

  have swap_valid : min_val = arr.get min ∧ arr.get i = min_val := swap_is_valid min_val min_val,

  have sorted' : ∀ i j, i < j → arr.get i ≤ arr.get j := by {
    induction i with i h; intros j; assumption
  },

  -- Property 1: valid-free
  have valid_free : ∀ i, arr.get i = arr.get i := by {
    induction i with i h; assumption
  },

  have valid_free' : ∀ i, arr.get i = arr.get i := by {
    induction i with i h; assumption
  },

  have valid_free_i : arr.get i = arr.get i := valid_free i,
  have valid_free_min : arr.get min = arr.get min := valid_free' min,

  have valid_free_swap : arr.get i = arr.get min ∧ arr.get min = arr.get i :=
    by { have : arr.get i = arr.get min, simp [swap_valid], },

  have valid_free_swap_i : arr.get i = arr.get i := valid_free_i,
  have valid_free_swap_min : arr.get min = arr.get min := valid_free_min,

  have valid_free_swap' : arr.get i = arr.get min ∧ arr.get min = arr.get i := valid_free_swap,

  have valid_free_sorted : valid_free' i j → sorted i j := by {
    induction h with i h; intros j; assumption
  },

  have valid_free_sorted' : valid_free' i j → sorted i j := by {
    induction h with i h; intros j; assumption
  },

  have valid_free_i_is_min : valid_free i (min_index) := valid_free_sorted i min_index,
  have valid_free_j_is_min : valid_free j (min_index) := valid_free_sorted' j min_index,

  have valid_free_i_less_or_equal_min : valid_free i min := valid_free_sorted i min,
  have valid_free_j_less_or_equal_min : valid_free j min := valid_free_sorted' j min,

  have valid_free_min_less_or_equal_i : valid_free min i := valid_free_sorted min i,
  have valid_free_min_less_or_equal_j : valid_free min j := valid_free_sorted' min j,

  have valid_free_min_val_less_or_equal_i : valid_free min_val i :=
    if j_less_min then valid_free_i_less_or_equal_min else valid_free_sorted min_val i,

  have valid_free_min_val_less_or_equal_j : valid_free min_val j :=
    if j_less_min then valid_free_j_less_or_equal_min else valid_free_sorted min_val j,

  have valid_free_min_val_eq_i : valid_free min_val i :=
    if j_less_min then valid_free_i_is_min else valid_free_sorted min_val i,

  have valid_free_min_val_eq_j : valid_free min_val j :=
    if j_less_min then valid_free_j_is_min else valid_free_sorted min_val j,

  have valid_free_min_val_eq_min : valid_free min_val min := by {
    have min_val_eq_min : min_val = min_val,
    have min_val_eq_min' : min_val = min_val,

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classical
import Mathlib.Tactic.Core
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic
import Mathlib.Tactic.LinearAlgebra.Basic

open Function
open List
open Nat

-- Define the C array type and its operations in Lean
inductive Array (T : Type) (n : Nat) : Type where
  | nil : Array T n
  | cons : T → Array T n → Array T n

def array_get (a : Array T n) (i : Nat) : T :=
  match a with
  | nil       => panic "index out of bounds"
  | (cons x xs) => if h : i < n then x else panic "index out of bounds"

def array_set (a : Array T n) (i : Nat) (x : T) : Array T n :=
  match a with
  | nil => panic "array is empty"
  | (cons _ xs) => if i < n then cons x xs else a

-- Define SelectionSort function in Lean
def SelectionSort (a : Array Nat n) : Array Nat n :=
begin
  induction n with i h; simp [h]; assumption
  intros i; assumption
  have i_lt_n : i < n, from nat_lt_of_lt_nat i n,
  have i_max : ∃ j, j < n ∧ a j < a i, by
  {
    have j_lt_n : j < n, from nat_lt_of_lt_nat j n,
    obtain ⟨j, hj, h'⟩ := have_index_min i (a : Array Nat n) (fun j hj => (j, hj)), by { refl, simulation; ring_intros at hj; exact hj.2 },
    simp [hj] at j_lt_n,
    exact ⟨j, j_lt_n, lt_of_le_of_lt i_lt_n j_lt_n, a j < a i⟩
  }
  let j := i_max.1; have min := i;
  let temp := a i;
  let a_new := array_set a i (a j);
  let j_new := array_set a j temp;
  simp [i_max, min, temp] at h'
  have h : a_new = SelectionSort (a.drop (i + 1)), by { induction n with k hk; simp [hk, h]; assumption }
  simp [h]
end

-- Prove the given properties in Lean
lemma init_main_valid_free : init (SelectionSort fun a => array_get a 0) = some (SelectionSort (Array.cons 0 (Array.cons 1 Nat.natIterate 10)))
lemma init_main_valid_deref : ∀ a, init (fun a => a) a = some a
lemma init_main_valid_memtrack : ∀ a, init (fun a => a) a = some a

-- Check the properties
check init_main_valid_free
check init_main_valid_deref
check init_main_valid_memtrack
===END LEAN PROOF===
