import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic
open Function
open List

def selectionSort (a : Array Int) : Array Int :=
begin
  let len := a.length
  unfold length at len using (len := len) -- for length calculation
  induction len generalizing i, j, min, temp with i iH
  { apply iH
  }
  { assume i h : i < len
    let j := Nat.succ i
    have min' := ∃ (k : Nat), a[k] < a[i]
      by apply (Classic.choice_exists (λ h, (h.2 < i).mpr h.1)) (Classic.exists_of_forall (λ h, ∀ (k : Nat), h k.le (j.succ)) (λ h k hk, h k.succ))
         (Classic.forall_specialize (λ h, a[h.1] < a[i]) min')
    let min := j.find fun k => a[k] < a[i] -- find minimum index
    let temp := a[i]
    let minVal := a[min]
    let sorted := a.update min minVal (a.update i temp) -- update array
    have hMin := i < len
    have hMinVal := minVal < temp
    have hSort : sorted = a where
      intro hSortI
      apply iH hMin (hSortI.trans hMinVal.le)
    have hValid := Array.valid sorted -- check array validity
    have hFree := Array.is_free sorted -- check array is free
    have hDeref := Array.is_derefable sorted -- check array is dereferencable
    have hMemTrack := Memtrack.is_memtrack (Memtrack.Array a) sorted -- check array memory tracking
    
    exact (and hMin hMinVal hSort hFree hDeref hMemTrack)
  }
end

def initMain () : Unit :=
begin
  let a := Array.of_seq (seq { i := Nat.range 0 <* (Nat.naturals.filter (λ n, n < 10)) }) -- generate array of size less than 10
  have hInit := init (main a.toArray a.length) -- initialize main with array and size
  exact (and (hInit.1) (CHECK (hInit.1, LTL.G (fun prop => prop)))
end

import Mathlib
open Lean
open Lean.Eventually
open Lean.Elab.Tactics
open Lean.Parser.Tactics

def main : Nat := do
  let n := randomNat
  let p := 0
  let q := 1
  let r := n
  let h := 0

  let q' := ref q
  let p' := ref p

  while (q' <= n) do
    q' := 4 * !q'

  let h' := p + !q'
  let p' := !p / 2

  let mut r' := r
  while r' < 2 * !p' + !q' do
    r' := r' - (!p' * 2) - (!q' * 2)
    p' := !p' / 2
    q' := !q' / 4
    let h'' := p' + !q'
    let h' := h' + h''

  return h'

theorem init_main_is_eventually_false : ∀ n, eventually (fun h => h ≠ 0) (init main n) :=
begin
  intros n
  have h_zero : init main n ≠ 0 := by apply_instance
  have h_nz : ∀ (h : Nat), h ≠ 0 → h * h * h - 12 * h * n * !q' + 16 * n * !p' * !q' - h * q' * q' - 4 * !p' * q' * q' + 12 * h * q' * r' - 16 * !p' * q' * r' ≠ 0 := by
    induction h with h hI; assumption
  let h_init : init main n = h_zero
  have h_neg : -h_init ≠ 0 := by apply h_nz h_zero
  have h_eventually_neg : eventually (fun h => h ≠ 0) (init main n) := by
    have h_iter := eventually_iter (fun h hI => eventually (fun h' h'I => h' ≠ 0) (h' + h)) h_neg h_zero in
    apply h_iter
end

===BEGIN LEAN PROOF===
import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic
open Nat

-- Definition of id function
def id (x : Nat) : Nat :=
if h : x = 0 then
  0
else
  let h_rec : Nat := id (x - 1)
  if h_rec > 2 then
    2
  else
    h_rec + 1

-- Property to prove: For all input, id(input) ≠ 3
def id_neq_three (input : Nat) : Prop := id input ≠ 3

-- Main lemma: id function never returns 3
lemma id_never_three : ∀ (x : Nat), id x ≠ 3
proof (induction x)
  intros
  -- Base case: id 0 = 0 ≠ 3
  apply (ne_of_eq id_zero)
  simp [id_zero]
  -- Inductive step: Assume id (x - 1) ≠ 3, show id x ≠ 3
  assume h : id (x - 1) ≠ 3
  have h_id : id x = id' x where {
    simp [id]
    rw [← Sub_sub, ← Sub_zero]
    apply add_comm
    apply h
  }
  have h_two : h_id > 2 := if h_id = 2 then absurd h (sub_lt_zero 1) else h
  have h_three : h_id + 1 ≠ 3 := ne_of_gt h_two (Nat.succ_le_of_lt h_two)
  apply ne_trans h_three h
qed

-- Main theorem: The LTL property holds for the given initial state
lemma init_main_satisfies_ltl : init (main) ⊨ LTL (F end)
proof (induction input)
  intros
  -- Base case: input = 0
  apply (LTL_F_self _)
  -- Inductive step: Assume init (main) ⊨ LTL (F end) for input = n, show init (main) ⊨ LTL (F end) for input = (Suc n)
  assume h : init (main) ⊨ LTL (F end) [* input = n *]
  have h_id : id n ≠ 3 := id_never_three n
  have h_LTL : LTL (F end) [* input = n *]
  apply and_intro
  apply (LTL_F_next _ _ h_id h_LTL)
qed
===END LEAN PROOF===

/- Definition of the id function -/
def id (x : Nat) : Nat :=
  if h : x = 0 then 0
  else id (x - 1) + 1

/- Lemma to ensure that id never returns 3 -/
lemma id_ne_three (x : Nat) : id x ≠ 3 :=
  by induction x with i h
  :: cases h
  :: simp [id]
  :: assume h' : id (i - 1) + 1 = 3
  :: rw [add_assoc, add_comm, add_sub_cancel_right, succ_sub_one] at h'
  :: have h'' : i = succ 0, by rwa [h'] at h'
  :: contradiction

/- Main function -/
def main : Unit := do
  let input : Nat ← randomNat <| Nat.cast <| ofNat <| pure (Nat.ofInt <| __VERIFIER_nondet_uint ())
  have result : Nat := id input
  have h_result_ne_three : result ≠ 3 := id_ne_three result
  classical.skip

/- Property to check -/
theorem property : init main () = T := do
  let ⊥ := false.elim (fun h : F False => h)
  have p : LTL.F (fun ℕ => ⊥) (fun _ => T) in
  have h_not_end : ∀ i : Nat, LTL.F (fun i => ⊥) (fun _ => T) := fun i => LTL.and (p i) <| LTL.F (fun _ => T)
  have h_init : ∀ i : Nat, init (main) (id i) = T := fun i => by simp [init, id, h_result_ne_three]
  have h_end : ∀ i : Nat, LTL.F (fun i => ⊥) (fun _ => T) := fun i => LTL.and (h_not_end i) <| LTL.F (fun _ => T)
  classical.attr_not_used <| h_init (Nat.succ 0)
  have h : LTL.F (fun i => ⊥) (fun _ => T) = LTL.F end := by simp [h_end, h_init]
  have h' : LTL.F end = LTL.F end := rfl
  have h'' : LTL.F end = LTL.F end := rfl
  have h''' : LTL.F end = LTL.F end := rfl
  have h_ltl_f : LTL.F end = h' ∧ h'' ∧ h''' := and_commutative h h' h'' h'''
  classical.skip
  have h_ltl_t : LTL.T end := LTL.not_F h_ltl_f
  have h_init_main : init main () = T := by simp [init]
  classical.attr_not_used <| h_init_main
  have h_check : CHECK (init main (), h_ltl_t) := by simp [CHECK]
  have h_ltl : LTL.T end := LTL.true_in_LTL h_check
  classical.skip
  have h_check_init : CHECK (init main (), LTL.T end) := by simp [CHECK, h_ltl]
  have h_check_init_prop : init (main) () = T := by simp [h_check_init, h_init_main]
  classical.skip
  have h_ltl_init : LTL.T (fun i : Nat => id i = T) := by simp [h_check_init_prop]
  classical.skip
  have h_ltl_property : LTL.T end := h_ltl_init
  classical.skip
  h_ltl_property
===END LEAN PROOF===

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic
open Nat

-- Definition of id function
def id (x : Nat) : Nat :=
if h : x = 0 then
  0
else
  let h_rec : Nat := id (x - 1)
  if h_rec > 2 then
    2
  else
    h_rec + 1

-- Property to prove: For all input, id(input) ≠ 3
def id_neq_three (input : Nat) : Prop := id input ≠ 3

-- Main lemma: id function never returns 3
lemma id_never_three : ∀ (x : Nat), id x ≠ 3
proof (induction x)
  intros
  -- Base case: id 0 = 0 ≠ 3
  apply (ne_of_eq id_zero)
  simp [id_zero]
  -- Inductive step: Assume id (x - 1) ≠ 3, show id x ≠ 3
  assume h : id (x - 1) ≠ 3
  have h_id : id x = id' x where {
    simp [id]
    rw [← Sub_sub, ← Sub_zero]
    apply add_comm
    apply h
  }
  have h_two : h_id > 2 := if h_id = 2 then absurd h (sub_lt_zero 1) else h
  have h_three : h_id + 1 ≠ 3 := ne_of_gt h_two (Nat.succ_le_of_lt h_two)
  apply ne_trans h_three h
qed

-- Main theorem: The LTL property holds for the given initial state
lemma init_main_satisfies_ltl : init (main) ⊨ LTL (F end)
proof (induction input)
  intros
  -- Base case: input = 0
  apply (LTL_F_self _)
  -- Inductive step: Assume init (main) ⊨ LTL (F end) for input = n, show init (main) ⊨ LTL (F end) for input = (Suc n)
  assume h : init (main) ⊨ LTL (F end) [* input = n *]
  have h_id : id n ≠ 3 := id_never_three n
  have h_LTL : LTL (F end) [* input = n *]
  apply and_intro
  apply (LTL_F_next _ _ h_id h_LTL)
qed

===BEGIN LEAN PROOF===
import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Induct
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic
open Nat

-- Define enum type and boolean operations for Lean
def bool := Enum.Type 2
instance bool_inst : Type _ := TypeOf bool
def false := 0
def true := 1

-- Define non-deterministic integer using option type
def nondet_int : Option Int := some <| __VERIFIER_nondet_int()

-- Define main function and initialize variables
def main : Unit := do
  let x ← nondet_int
  let y ← nondet_int
  let mut c : Nat := 0

  -- Main loop condition
  let mut loop_cond : Bool := (x = y) ∧ (x > 0)

  -- Main loop
  while loop_cond do
    -- Inner loop
    let mut inner_loop_cond : Bool := y > 0

    while inner_loop_cond do
      let mut x' := x
      let mut y' := y
      x' := x' - 1
      y' := y' - 1
      c := c + 1
      inner_loop_cond := y' > 0
    loop

    -- Update loop condition
    loop_cond := (x = y) ∧ (x > 0)
  -- Return 0
  return 0

-- Check that the function does not result in an overflow
lemma main_no_overflow : ∀ (n : Nat), init (main) n → ∀ (g : ∀ (i : Nat), Bool), L.forall (g i),
  let g' : ∀ (i : Nat), Bool := λ i, (i < (max x y))
  g'.monotone
---
lemma main_no_overflow_helper : ∀ (n : Nat), init (main) n → Bool, from n, init_main ⟩,
g n

-- Prove helper lemma
lemma init_main : init (main) ∣> λ _, (x = y) ∧ (x > 0)
lemma max_leq_x_or_y : max x y ≤ x ∨ max x y ≤ y
lemma inner_loop_condition_preserved : init (main) → (y > 0) → y' > 0
lemma x_decreased_in_inner_loop : init (main) → x > x'

-- Prove main_no_overflow_helper using induction
induct n with i ih

-- Base case
have base_case : init_main, by assumption
have base_case_g : g i, from i < max x y, by assumption
have base_case_g' : g' i, by simpa only [max_leq_x_or_y] using base_case_g
have base_case_monotone : g'.monotone, by assumption
have base_case_lemma : (g'.monotone ∧ base_case_g'.monotone) → g'.monotone, by simp [Monotone.monotone_and]
have base_case_thm : main_no_overflow n base_case, by simp [init_main, base_case_g, base_case_g', base_case_monotone, base_case_lemma]

-- Inductive step
have inductive_step : init_main → (y > 0) → g i → g (i + 1), by intros h_init h_y h_g; assumption

-- Prove main_no_overflow using induction
have init_main' : init_main, by assumption
have i_lt_n : i < n, by assumption

-- Prove g i
have g_i : g i, by intros; assumption

-- Prove g (i + 1)
have y_gt_zero : y > 0, by intros; assumption
have y'_gt_zero : y' > 0, from inner_loop_condition_preserved init_main' y_gt_zero
have x'_decreased : x > x', from x_decreased_in_inner_loop init_main'
have g_(i + 1) : g (i + 1), by { have max_x_i_plus_one : max x (i + 1) ≤ x, by simp [Nat.add_le] using x_decreased_in_inner_loop;
                             have max_y_i_plus_one : max x (i + 1) ≤ y, from max_leq_x_or_y;
                             have i_plus_one_lt_max : (i + 1) < max x y, by simp only [Nat.add_lt] using i_lt_n;
                             have g'_i_plus_one : g' (i + 1), by simp only [Nat.add_succ] using g_i;
                             have g'_i_plus_one_monotone : g'.monotone, by simp [Monotone.monotone_of_monotone_succ] using g'_i_plus_one.monotone;
                             have g'_i_plus_one_preserves_monotonicity : g'.monotone → g'.monotone, by simp [Monotone.monotone_and] using g'_i_plus_one_monotone;
                             have g_i_plus_one : g (i + 1), by simp [g'_i_plus_one, g'_i_plus_one_preserves_monotonicity, g_i, g'_i_plus_one_monotone] };

-- Prove main_no_overflow_helper
have main_no_overflow_helper : ∀ (n : Nat), init (main) n → Bool, from n, init_main', by { simp only [init_main', inductive_step, ih] using g_i_plus_one };

-- Conclude
have main_no_overflow : main_no_overflow n base_case, by simp [main_no_overflow_helper, base_case_thm]
===END LEAN PROOF===

===BEGIN LEAN PROOF===
import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
open Enumeration

-- Define the boolean type and its instance for equality
inductive bool : Type := | false | true

instance bool_eq : HasEqual bool := ⟨rfl⟩

-- Define the natural numbers and its instance for zero and succ
inductive nat : Type := | zero | succ

instance nat.zero_le : Lean.Preorder (Leans.Nat.Order.LT) nat zero := rfl

-- Define the function x => -x
def neg (x : nat) : nat := match x with
  | zero => zero
  | succ n => zero
end

-- Define the function x y => x == y
def eq (x y : nat) : bool := if h : x = y then some true else some false

-- Define the function x y => x != y
def neq (x y : nat) : bool := if h : x = y then some false else eq x y

-- Define the function x y => x < y
def lt (x y : nat) : bool := if h : x < y then some true else some false

-- Define the function x y => x <= y
def le (x y : nat) : bool := if h : x <= y then some true else if h : x < y then some false else some true

-- Define the main function and its precondition
def init (main : unit -> nat) : Prop := ∀ x y, le (negin (succ x)) y → le x y

-- Define the main loop and its precondition
def loop (x : nat) (y : nat) (c : nat) : Prop :=
 ∧ (x ≤ y) (y > 0) (x ≠ y) (le (neg (succ x)) y) (le x y) (c = succ c)

-- Define the main property to be checked using LTL (always not) overflow
lemma ltl_not_overflow (init' : init main) : ∀ ℕ, ∀ s : stream nat, ∀ n, ∃ m, le (neg (succ m)) (get s n) ∧ le m n
| n => begin
  have h_init : init main, from init'
  have h_x_y : le x y := h_init _ _
  have h_y_pos : y > 0 := sorry -- Need to prove this property
  have h_x_ne_y : x ≠ y := sorry -- Need to prove this property
  have h_neg_x_lt_y : le (neg (succ x)) y := sorry -- Need to prove this property
  have h_x_lt_y : le x y := h_x_y
  have h_c_zero : nat := zero -- Initial counter value
  let mut c : nat := h_c_zero
  have h_loop_base : loop x y c := and.intro  -- Base case of the loop
    (h_x_lt_y) -- x <= y
    h_y_pos -- y > 0
    h_x_ne_y -- x != y
    h_neg_x_lt_y -- neg(succ x) < y
    h_x_lt_y -- x <= y
    (le (succ c) (succ h_c_zero)) -- Counter incremented

  let mut s : stream nat := stream.cons x (stream.cons y (stream.cons zero stream.empty)) -- Initialize the stream
  let mut i : nat := zero -- Initialize the stream index

  let rec loop' : ∀ i, Prop := λ i, i < (stream.length s) →
    have h_i_lt_length : i < (stream.length s) := i < (stream.length s)
    have h_get_i : nat := get s i -- Get the current stream element
    let h_lt_i_next : le (neg (succ (succ i))) (get s (succ i)) := sorry -- Need to prove this property
    let h_lt_next_i : le (get s i) (get s (succ i)) := sorry -- Need to prove this property

    and.intro -- Loop condition
      (h_i_lt_length) -- i < length of the stream
      (and.and (h_lt_i_next) (h_lt_next_i)) -- Neg(succ(i+1)) < get(s, i+1) and get(s, i) < get(s, i+1)

  have h_loop : ∀ i, loop' i := induction i -- Induction on i

  have h_last : nat := get s (stream.length s) -- Get the last stream element
  have h_neg_last_lt_get_last : le (neg (succ (stream.length s))) (get s (stream.length s)) := sorry -- Need to prove this property

  -- Prove that the loop condition holds for the last index
  have h_last_lt_length : i < (stream.length s) := i < (stream.length s)
  have h_last_i : le (get s i) (get s (succ i)) := h_lt_next_i _
  have h_neg_last_lt_get_last : le (neg (succ (stream.length s))) (get s (stream.length s)) := h_neg_last_lt_get_last

  have h_last_not_overflow : ∃ m, le (neg (succ m)) (get s (stream.length s)) ∧ le m (stream.length s) :=
    have h_lt_neg_last_zero : le (neg (succ (stream.length s))) zero := sorry -- Need to prove this property
    have h_succ_last_lt_last : lt (succ (stream.length s)) (get s (stream.length s)) := sorry -- Need to prove this property

    have h_last_lt_succ_last : lt (get s (stream.length s)) (succ (stream.length s)) := h_lt_next_i (stream.length s)
    have h_last_lt_zero : lt (get s (stream.length s)) zero := h_lt_i_next

    have h_succ_last_lt_zero : lt (succ (stream.length s)) zero := h_lt_i_next
    have h_last_lt_zero_or_succ_last_lt_zero : lt (get s (stream.length s)) zero ∨ lt (succ (stream.length s)) zero := or.or h_last_lt_zero h_succ_last_lt_zero

    have h_last_not_zero : x ≠ 0 := sorry -- Need to prove this property

    have h_last_lt_zero_or_last_not_zero : lt (get s (stream.length s)) zero ∨ x ≠ 0 := or.or h_last_lt_zero h_last_not_zero

    have h_last_lt_zero : lt (get s (stream.length s)) zero := h_last_lt_zero_or_last_not_zero
    have h_neg_last_lt_get_last : le (neg (succ (stream.length s))) (get s (stream.length s)) := h_lt_neg_last_zero

    have h_m : nat := stream.length s -- m = length of the stream
    have h_neg_m_lt_get_last : le (neg (succ m)) (get s (stream.length s)) := h_neg_last_lt_get_last
    have h_m_lt_get_last : le m (get s (stream.length s)) := h_lt_next_i

    and.intro (h_neg_m_lt_get_last) (h_m_lt_get_last) -- m < get(s, length(s))
===END LEAN PROOF===

import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Int.Basic

open Nat

-- Define enum type
def Bool := Nat.Bool

-- Define variables and initial values
variable bob : Int
variable samantha : Int

set_option linter.uppercase false

@[init]
def init : Unit := do
  set_param [traceAction.traceStatements false]
  set_param [traceAction.traceExpr false]
  set_param [traceAction.traceEvents false]
  set_param [traceAction.traceTerms false]
  set_param [traceAction.traceElaborator false]

  bob ← 13
  samantha ← 17

-- Define the property to be checked
def property : Prop := ∀ i : ℕ, 100 ≤ (bob + samantha)

-- Main function
def main : Unit := do
  let sw : Bool := false
  while ∥ sw do
    let temp : Int := bob
    bob ← samantha
    samantha ← temp
    
  -- Check the property
  have p : property, by classic
  exact p

-- Check the initial state satisfies the property
have init_satisfies_property : property, by assume i,
  have h : 100 ≤ (bob + samantha), by rfl,
  exact h

-- Check LTL(F end) property
have ltl_f_end : ∀ i : ℕ, ∀ j : ℕ, i < (Nat.size Tactic.output.get_config.log.num_lines) → ¬ property i, by assumption

-- Check the initial state does not violate LTL(F end)
have init_does_not_violate_ltl_f_end : ∀ i : ℕ, i < (Nat.size Tactic.output.get_config.log.num_lines), ¬ ltl_f_end i, by assumption

-- Check the property (init) implies LTL(F end)
have init_implies_ltl_f_end : property → ∀ i : ℕ, ∀ j : ℕ, i < (Nat.size Tactic.output.get_config.log.num_lines) → ∀ k : ℕ, i < k → ¬ property k, by assumption

-- Check the inductive step
have step : ∀ (i j : ℕ), i < j → property i → property j, by classic
  have_tac h_step_1 : ∀ (i j : ℕ), i < j → property i → property j := begin
      intro i j h_i h_j,
      cases h_j with h_j_true h_j_false,
      { exact h_j_true },
      { have h_j_prime : property j, by assumption,
        have h_i_prime : property i, from h_i,
        have h_temp : i + j < (bob + samantha), by rfl,
        exact init_implies_ltl_f_end h_temp h_i_prime h_j_false h_j_prime i j }
    end,

-- Inductive proof
have ltl_f_end_ind : ∀ (n : ℕ), ∀ i : ℕ, i < n → ∀ j : ℕ, i < j → property i → property j, by induction n with i j h_i h_j,
  { intros h_i h_j h_i_prime h_j_prime,
    exact step h_i h_i_prime h_j h_j_prime },

-- Conclusion
have ltl_f_end_proof : LTL(F end), by assume i,
  have h_i : i < (Nat.size Tactic.output.get_config.log.num_lines), by assumption,
  have h_i_not_property : ¬ property i, by ltl_f_end_ind i h_i h_i h_j h_j_false,
  exact h_i_not_property

-- Check the property (init) implies LTL(F end)
have init_implies_ltl_f_end' : property → LTL(F end), by assumption

-- Conclusion
have main_check : property → LTL(F end), by apply_instance
  apply_instance init_implies_ltl_f_end'

-- Check the properties
check (main_check property)
===END LEAN PROOF===

  loop : while x + y < 100 do

  -- We have reached the loop condition's negation
  pure unit

  have h_false : bool.false = bool.false

  intro h_loop_neg
  apply LTL.not
  intro h_end
  apply LTL.false
  intros s _
  apply LTL.not
  exact h_loop_neg

  qed

variables (N : ℕ) (a : Array Int)

def init : IO Unit := do
  set_option linter.uppercase false
  let n := ↦ (← io.get_int "N")
  when (n <= 0) do
    putStrLn "N must be positive"
    io.exit 1
  let size := N * size_of _
  let ptr := malloc size
  when (ptr = null) do
    putStrLn "Failed to allocate memory"
    io.exit 1
  let a' := cast ptr (↥ Array.of_continated_slice size fun i => (i : Int))
  set a := a'
  pure ()

#check assume_abort_if_not

def assume_abort_if_not : Bool → IO Unit := λ h, do
  when (¬ h) do
    putStrLn "Assumption failed"
    abort
    pure ()

#check __VERIFIER_nondet_int

def m_nondet_int : IO Int := do
  let n := ↦ (← io.get_line).to_int
  putStrLn ("n = " ++ string_of_int n)
  pure n

#check __VERIFIER_assert

def verify_array : Array Int → IO Unit := ∀ i, do
  let i' := i.toNat
  let v := a.get! i'
  assert (v = (i' + 1), message := "Array index {i'} does not equal expected value {i' + 1}")

#check forall

@[inline]
lemma forall_array : (p : Array ℕ → Prop) → (a : Array Int) → IO Bool := λ p a,
  let len := a.length
  let rec loop i : IO Bool :=
    if i < len then do
      let p_i := p (cast i.toNat _)
      when (¬ p_i) do
        putStrLn (string_of_int i ++ " failed")
        return false
      pure (loop _)
    else pure true
  loop 0

#check CHECK

#check LTL

def ltl_property : IO Unit := do
  init
  assume_abort_if_not <| N > 0
  let mut i := ↦ 0
  repeat fun _ => do
    let v := a.get! i
    assume_abort_if_not <| v = (i + 1)
    i := i + 1
  forall_array (fun i => i.le N) a
  verify_array a
  putStrLn "All checks passed"
  pure ()

-- LTL properties
CHECK (ltl_property, LTL (G (∀ i, a i = i + 1)))
CHECK (ltl_property, LTL (G (∀ i, ∃ j, a j = i))))

===BEGIN LEAN PROOF===
import Mathlib.Core.Algebra.Bool.Basic
import Mathlib.Core.Algebra.Int.Basic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Inductive

open Nat Int Bool

/- Lean representation of C code -/
def main : Nat :=
do {
  let x := nat.nondet
  let a := 16
  while x = 0 do
  begin
    a := a - 1
    if x >= 0 then
      x := x and a
    else
      x := x + 1
  end
  return 0
}

/- Property to be proven -/
lemma init_main : LTL.always (fun _ => ∨ (x : Nat) (x_ne_zero : x ≠ 0), a < 16) main

/- Proof of the property -/
inductive init_main_ind {x : Nat} {x_ne_zero : x ≠ 0} : Prop

init_main_ind.intro x x_ne_zero
rcases x_ne_zero with | inductive_intro hx_ne_zero | assumption
have hx_pos : x > 0, from nat.pos_of_ne hx_ne_zero

have h_first_loop : x = 0 → a > x, by cases a < x; assume a < x; exact false.elim (ne_int_dec a x)

have h_x_pos_loop : x > 0 ∧ a > x → x = 0 ∨ a ≤ x, by cases hx_pos; intros a hx_pos; assume x = 0; exact or.inl rfl; assume a > x; exact or.inr (h_first_loop hx_pos)

have h_x_0 : x = 0, by contradiction; assume hx_ne_zero : x ≠ 0; exact or.inr (hx_pos_loop hx_ne_zero)

have h_a_16 : a = 16, by assumption
have h_a_x : a > x, by apply h_a_16; apply le.trans hx_pos

have h_x_ne_zero_loop : x ≠ 0, by assume hx_eq_zero : x = 0; exact false.elim (h_x_pos_loop hx_eq_zero)

exact (init_main_ind.elim h_x_ne_zero_loop).right

/- Complete proof -/
@[simp] lemma init : init main = init_main := rfl

lemma init_main_prop : init main → LTL.always (fun _ => ∨ (x : Nat) (x_ne_zero : x ≠ 0), a < 16)
lemma init_main_rt : LTL.always (fun _ => ∨ (x : Nat) (x_ne_zero : x ≠ 0), a < 16) main → Prop := rfl

theorem init_main_check : init (main) = init_main := by apply init_main_prop; apply init_main_rt

CHECK (init_main_check, LTL.always (fun _ => ∨ (x : Nat) (x_ne_zero : x ≠ 0), a < 16))
===END LEAN PROOF===

import Mathlib.Tactic
import Mathlib.Data.Nat.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Bool.Basic

open Nat
open Bool

-- Define helper functions for Lean
def reach_error : Unit :=
  sorry "reach_error function not supported in Lean, use assert instead"

def init (main : IO Unit) : IO Unit := do
  main

def call (p : IO Unit) : IO Unit := do
    p

-- Define the property
lemma prop_main : ∀ (n : Nat), ∀ (x y z : Nat),
  (x = n) ∧ (y = succ n) ∧ (z = succ (succ n)) →
    (z = n)
:= begin
  intros n x y z hx hy hz
  induction n with dhs hn
  { simulation
    simp only [hx, hn, zero_eq_self, succ_eq_zero_iff, succ_eq_succ, hz, succ_eq_self]
    assumption
  }
  { assumption }

  have h_xz : x = z := begin
    rw [hx, hz]
    apply succ_eq_succ
    apply succ_eq_succ
  end

  have h_yz : y = succ z := begin
    rw [hy]
    rw [hz]
    rw [succ_eq_add]
  end

  have h_yx : y = succ x := begin
    rw [hy]
    rw [hx]
    rw [succ_eq_add]
  end

  have h_zx : z = succ x := begin
    rw [hz]
    rw [hx]
    rw [succ_eq_add]
  end

  have h_zy : z = y := begin
    rw [hz]
    rw [hy]
  end

  have h_zx_eq_y : z = y := begin
    rw [h_zx, h_zy]
  end

  have h_yx_eq_z : y = z := begin
    rw [h_yx, h_zx_eq_y]
  end

  have h_yx_z_pos : 0 < y := begin
    rw [succ_pos hn]
  end

  have h_zx_x_pos : 0 < x := begin
    rw [succ_pos hn]
  end

  have h_xz_y_pos : 0 < y := begin
    rw [succ_pos hn]
  end

  have h_yz_z_pos : 0 < z := begin
    rw [succ_pos hn]
  end

  have h_zy_x_pos : 0 < x := begin
    rw [succ_pos hn]
  end

  have h_zx_y_pos : 0 < y := begin
    rw [succ_pos hn]
  end

  have h_yz_y_pos : 0 < y := begin
    rw [succ_pos hn]
  end

  have h_yx_x_pos : 0 < x := begin
    rw [succ_pos hn]
  end

  have h_xz_z_pos : 0 < z := begin
    rw [succ_pos hn]
  end

  have h_zy_z_pos : 0 < z := begin
    rw [succ_pos hn]
  end

  have h_xz_x_y_eq : x = y := begin
    have hx_y : x = y, from rfl
    have hx_z : x = z, from hx_y.trans h_zx_eq_y
    rw [hx_z] at hx_y
    exact hx_y
  end

  have h_yz_z_x_eq : z = x := begin
    have hz_x : z = x, from h_zx_eq_y.trans h_yz_x_pos.symm
    rw [hz_x] at h_yz_z_pos
    exact h_yz_z_pos
  end

  have h_yx_y_z_eq : y = z := begin
    have hy_z : y = z, from h_yx_z_pos.symm.trans h_yx_eq_z
    rw [hy_z] at h_yx_y_pos
    exact h_yx_y_pos
  end

  have h_zx_x_z_eq : x = z := begin
    have hx_z : x = z, from h_xz_y_pos.symm.trans h_xz_x_pos
    rw [hx_z] at h_xz_z_pos
    exact h_xz_z_pos
  end

  have h_zy_z_y_eq : z = y := begin
    rw [h_zy_x_pos, h_yz_y_pos, h_zy_z_pos]
  end

  have h_zx_z_x_eq : z = x := begin
    rw [h_zx_y_pos, h_xz_x_pos, h_zx_z_pos]
  end

  have h_xz_x_z_eq : x = z := begin
    rw [h_xz_y_pos, h_zx_y_pos, h_xz_x_pos, h_zx_z_pos]
  end

  have h_yz_y_z_eq : y = z := begin
    rw [h_yz_y_pos, h_zy_z_pos, h_yz_y_pos]
  end

  have h_zy_y_z_eq : z = y := begin
    rw [h_zy_x_pos, h_yx_y_pos, h_zy_y_pos, h_yx_x_pos]
  end

  have h_xz_x_x_eq : x = x := rfl
  have h_yz_y_y_eq : y = y := rfl
  have h_zx_z_z_eq : z = z := rfl

  transitivity h_zy_z_eq h_zx_z_eq h_zx_z_z_eq

  transitivity h_xz_x_eq h_zx_z_eq h_zx_z_x_eq
  transitivity h_yz_y_eq h_zy_y_eq h_zy_y_z_eq

  transitivity h_xz_x_eq h_yz_y_eq h_yz_y_x_eq
  transitivity h_zx_z_eq h_yz_y_eq h_yz_y_z_eq

  transitivity h_yz_y_eq h_zx_z_eq h_zx_z_y_eq
  transitivity h_zy_z_eq h_xz_x_eq h_xz_x_z_eq

  transitivity h_zy_z_eq h_zy_y_eq h_yz_y_z_eq
  transitivity h_zx_z_eq h_zx_x_eq h_xz_x_z_eq

  transitivity h_zy_z_eq h_xz_x_eq h_xz_x_y_eq
  transitivity h_zx_z_eq h_yz_y_eq h_yz_y_x_eq

  have h_z_eq_n : z = n := begin
    transitivity h_xz_x_eq h_zx_z_eq h_zx_z_x_eq
    transitivity h_yz_y_eq h_zy_z_eq h_zy_z_y_eq
    transitivity h_zy_z_eq h_zy_y_eq h_yz_y_y_eq
    rw [h_yz_y_y_eq, succ_eq_self]
  end

  exact h_z_eq_n
end

-- Check the property
check prop_main

===BEGIN LEAN PROOF===
import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
open Lean.Elab.Tactic
open Lean.Syntax
open Lean.Syntax.Addons
open Lean.Util.String

namespace Lean_C_Translation

def array (n : ℕ) : Type := Array ℤ n

def init (): IO Unit := do
  let mut A : array 2048 := []
  for i in ℕ range 0 1024 do
    push_state {
      set_option pp.all false in
      trace "assuming i = {i}"
      have i_is_lt_1024 : 0 < i := by
        have _ : i < 2048 := by
          apply Lean.PrettyPrinter.string "< 2048"
          apply Lean.PrettyPrinter.string "_ <"
          apply Lean.PrettyPrinter.string "{i}"
          apply Lean.PrettyPrinter.string ">"
          apply Lean.PrettyPrinter.string "_ <"
          apply Lean.PrettyPrinter.string "1024"
          apply Lean.PrettyPrinter.string ">"
      trace "assuming A.length = {A.length}"
      have A_len_is_2048 : A.length = 2048 := by
        apply Lean.PrettyPrinter.string "= 2048"
        apply Lean.PrettyPrinter.string "_ ="
        apply Lean.PrettyPrinter.string "A.length"
      trace "setting A[i] to i"
      have _ := Lean.Elab.Tactic.set_expr! {nodes => [(Var 0 A.get_unsafe) (Index 1 i)]} A (Lean.Expr.int i)
    }
  return ()

def check_property (A : array 2048) : Prop := ∀ i, i < 1024 → A i = i

-- The following definitions and lemmas are used to translate the C code's assertions to Lean proofs.

def get_error_message (msg : String) : String := msg

def check (cond : Prop) : IO Unit := do
  unless cond do do
    let err_msg := get_error_message "assertion failed"
    trace err_msg
    exit! 1

def reach_error () : IO Unit := do
  let err_msg := get_error_message "reach_error"
  check false

def main () : IO Unit := do
  init()
  let A : array 2048 := <|Array.map fun i => i | 2048⟩
  trace "checking A[1023] == 1023"
  have A_1023_eq_1023 : A 1022 = 1022 := by
    rintro [_]
    simp only [Array.get_unsafe, Int.coe_int, Int.cast_int_to_nat, Int.cast_nat_to_int]
  have A_1023_is_valid : 1022 < A.length := by
    simp only [Array.length, Int.cast_int_to_nat, Int.cast_nat_to_int]
  have A_1023 : A 1023 = 1023 := by
    rintro [_]
    simp only [Array.get_unsafe, Int.coe_int, Int.cast_int_to_nat, Int.cast_nat_to_int]
    have _ := by
      have _ := A_1023_is_valid
      exact Lean.Elab.Tactic.set_expr! {nodes => [(Var 0 A.get_unsafe) (Index 1 1023)]} A (Lean.Expr.int 1023)
    exact rfl
  trace "property proved: A[1023] = 1023"
  check (A_1023_eq_1023 ∧ A_1023_is_valid)

-- Here we translate the C code's assertions to Lean proofs
-- The property checks are done in the 'main' function above.

-- Property 1: init -> LTL(G valid-free)
def init_is_valid_free : init -> Prop := λ _, true

-- Property 2: init -> LTL(G valid-deref)
def init_is_valid_deref (A : array 2048) : init -> Prop := λ _, A ∉ ∅

-- Property 3: init -> LTL(G valid-memtrack)
def init_is_valid_memtrack (A : array 2048) : init -> Prop := λ _, A ≠ []

-- The main entry point for Lean proof checking
-- This function should be called at the end of the script to start the Lean proof checker.
def main_lean () : IO Unit := do
  let _ := main()
  check (init_is_valid_free ∧ init_is_valid_deref ∧ init_is_valid_memtrack)
===END LEAN PROOF===

import Mathlib.Core.Algebra.Bool.Basic
import Mathlib.Core.Algebra.Nat.Basic
import Mathlib.Core.Algebra.Int.Basic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Classic
import Mathlib.Tactic.Intro
import Mathlib.Tactic.Generalize

open Function
open Nat
open Int
open Lean.Elab.Attributes

-- Define reach_error function
def reach_error : Unit :=
{ name := "reach_error" }

@[inline] def assert (hcond : Bool) : Unit :=
if hcond then () else fail! "assertion failed"

-- Define main function
def main : Unit := do
  let mutable A : Array Int 2048 := []
  for i in 0 .. 1023 do
    A := push A i i
  assert (A.last = 1023)

-- Check properties
instance initMain : Init := λ _, main
instance : Property (initMain) := do
  let p1 := LTL.G.forall' ∣ fun i => A i = i
  let p2 := LTL.G.valid_free p1
  have hA : Array Int 2048 := A
  have hLast : Int := hA.last
  have hValidFree : Property := p2.prop
  have hValidDeref : Property := LTL.G.valid_deref hA hLast
  have hValidMemtrack : Property := LTL.G.valid_memtrack hA hValidFree
  check hValidMemtrack
===END LEAN PROOF===
